ApplicationController - Core Application Reconciliation\n\n### Location\n- **Main Implementation**: `/workspace/controller/appcontroller.go` (96KB)\n- **Command Entry Point**: `/workspace/cmd/argocd-application-controller/commands/argocd_application_controller.go`\n- **Tests**: `/workspace/controller/appcontroller_test.go` (95KB)\n\n### Structure\nThe `ApplicationController` struct manages the reconciliation of Application CRDs:\n\n```\ntype ApplicationController struct {\n    cache                          *appstatecache.Cache\n    kubeClientset                  kubernetes.Interface\n    applicationClientset           appclientset.Interface\n    appRefreshQueue                workqueue.RateLimitingQueue\n    appComparisonTypeRefreshQueue  workqueue.RateLimitingQueue\n    appOperationQueue              workqueue.RateLimitingQueue\n    projectRefreshQueue            workqueue.RateLimitingQueue\n    appHydrateQueue                workqueue.RateLimitingQueue\n    hydrationQueue                 workqueue.RateLimitingQueue\n    appInformer                    cache.SharedIndexInformer\n    appStateManager                AppStateManager\n    stateCache                     statecache.LiveStateCache\n    metricsServer                  *metrics.MetricsServer\n    kubectlSemaphore               *semaphore.Weighted\n    clusterSharding                sharding.ClusterShardingCache\n    hydrator                       *hydrator.Hydrator\n}\n```\n\n### Key Methods\n- `Run()` - Main controller loop\n- `processAppRefreshQueueItem()` - Handles app refresh requests\n- `processAppOperationQueueItem()` - Executes sync operations\n- `processAppComparisonTypeRefreshQueueItem()` - Refreshes with specific comparison type\n- `processAppHydrateQueueItem()` - Manages application hydration\n- `processHydrationQueueItem()` - Processes hydration queue\n- `persistAppStatus()` - Persists application status\n- `autoSync()` - Initiates automatic sync operations\n- `shouldSelfHeal()` - Determines if self-healing is needed\n- `refreshAppConditions()` - Updates application conditions\n\n### Related Directories\n- `/workspace/controller/cache/` - LiveStateCache implementation for tracking cluster state\n- `/workspace/controller/hydrator/` - ApplicationHydrator for manifest hydration\n- `/workspace/controller/metrics/` - Metrics collection\n- `/workspace/controller/sharding/` - Cluster sharding logic\n- `/workspace/controller/utils/` - Utility functions\n\n---\n\n## 2. Sync and Diff Logic\n\n### Location\n- **Sync Implementation**: `/workspace/controller/sync.go` (24KB)\n- **Sync Tests**: `/workspace/controller/sync_test.go` (50KB)\n- **State Management**: `/workspace/controller/state.go` (49KB)\n- **State Tests**: `/workspace/controller/state_test.go` (66KB)\n- **Diff Utilities**: `/workspace/util/argo/diff/diff.go` (14KB)\n- **Diff Tests**: `/workspace/util/argo/diff/diff_test.go` (8KB)\n\n### Key Components\n\n#### AppStateManager Interface\nLocated in `/workspace/controller/state.go`, defines:\n\n```go\ntype AppStateManager interface {\n    CompareAppState(app, project, revisions, sources, ...) (*comparisonResult, error)\n    SyncAppState(app, state)\n    GetRepoObjs(app, sources, revisions, ...) ([]*Unstructured, []*ManifestResponse, bool, error)\n}\n```\n\n#### DiffConfigBuilder\nLocated in `/workspace/util/argo/diff/diff.go`:\n- Builds diff configurations with:\n  - Resource ignore rules\n  - Resource overrides\n  - Tracking methods\n  - Cache configuration\n  - Normalizer options\n\n#### comparisonResult Structure\nContains:\n- `syncStatus` - Current sync state\n- `healthStatus` - Application health\n- `resources` - Individual resource status\n- `managedResources` - Internal resource tracking\n- `reconciliationResult` - Sync reconciliation data\n- `diffConfig` - Diff configuration used\n- `timings` - Performance metrics\n- `diffResultList` - Detailed diff results\n\n### Sync Flow\n1. `CompareAppState()` - Compares desired vs live state\n2. `GetRepoObjs()` - Fetches target manifests from repo server\n3. `SyncAppState()` - Applies changes to cluster\n4. Diff computation using gitops-engine\n\n### Related Directories\n- `/workspace/util/argo/` - Core Argo utilities (46KB)\n- `/workspace/util/argo/diff/` - Diff/normalization logic\n- `/workspace/util/argo/normalizers/` - Resource normalizers\n- `/workspace/util/argo/managedfields/` - Kubernetes managed fields handling\n\n---\n\n## 4. Application Reconciliation Flow\n\n### Flow Diagram\n\n```\nApplication Resource\n        ↓\n[ApplicationController.Run()]\n        ↓\n[appRefreshQueue] ← Input events from K8s API\n        ↓\n[processAppRefreshQueueItem()]\n        ↓\n[appStateManager.CompareAppState()]\n        ↓\n┌────────────────────────────────────┐\n│ 1. Get Desired State (RepoServer)   │\n│    appStateManager.GetRepoObjs()    │\n│         ↓                           │\n│    [reposerver/repository.Service]  │\n│    - Clone/fetch git repo           │\n│    - Generate manifests             │\n│    - Return YAML objects            │\n├────────────────────────────────────┤\n│ 2. Get Live State (LiveStateCache)  │\n│    [controller/cache/LiveStateCache]│\n│    - Query cluster state            │\n│    - Track resource health          │\n├────────────────────────────────────┤\n│ 3. Compute Differences              │\n│    [util/argo/diff/DiffConfigBuilder]\n│    - Apply normalizers              │\n│    - Compare resources              │\n│    - Generate resource diffs        │\n└────────────────────────────────────┘\n        ↓\n[comparisonResult] → Application.Status.SyncStatus/HealthStatus\n        ↓\nDecision: Auto-sync or Manual?\n        ↓\nIf Auto-sync or Manual Sync Requested:\n        ↓\n[appOperationQueue]\n        ↓\n[processAppOperationQueueItem()]\n        ↓\n[appStateManager.SyncAppState()]\n        ↓\n┌──────────────────────────────────┐\n│ Execute Sync Operation:          │\n│ - Create sync waves              │\n│ - Apply/delete resources         │\n│ - Verify health                  │\n│ - Handle hooks                   │\n└──────────────────────────────────┘\n        ↓\n[Persist Operation State]\n        ↓\nApplication.Status.OperationState\n```\n\n### Key State Management Files\n\n#### `/workspace/controller/appcontroller.go`\n- Main reconciliation loop\n- Application watcher setup\n- Finalizer management\n- Status persistence\n- Operation handling\n\n#### `/workspace/controller/state.go`\n- CompareAppState implementation\n- GetRepoObjs implementation\n- manages all comparison logic\n- Builds resource trees\n- Handles resource tracking\n\n#### `/workspace/controller/sync.go`\n- SyncAppState implementation\n- Sync wave management\n- Diff application\n- Resource operation execution\n- Health verification\n\n---\n\n## 5. (other components)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\nThe Argo CD architecture follows a clear separation of concerns:\n\n1. **ApplicationController** (`/workspace/controller/`) - Orchestrates reconciliation, watches Applications, manages state and status\n2. **RepoServer** (`/workspace/reposerver/`) - Independently fetches git repos and generates manifests via gRPC\n3. **State Managers** (`/workspace/controller/state.go`, `sync.go`) - Compare desired vs live state and execute syncs\n4. **Diff Engine** (`/workspace/util/argo/diff/`) - Compares manifests with normalization and ignore rules\n5. **Cache Systems** (`/workspace/controller/cache/`, `/workspace/reposerver/cache/`) - Optimize performance through caching\n6. **API Server** (`/workspace/server/`) - Provides gRPC and REST interfaces for clients\n\nThe reconciliation flow is event-driven with multiple work queues for different operation types, enabling efficient batch processing and rate limiting."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a8863a4ffcee2043c (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)\n<usage>total_tokens: 56240\ntool_uses: 46\nduration_ms: 60229</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"61763242-f70f-4cb0-a3b8-fd590c2f9939","uuid":"1c5a4ba4-f37e-4d18-a0a3-7e09e4ed6a54","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Explore the Argo CD repository structure and identify the main packages/directories related to:\n1. Periodic Refresh (Time-based)**\n- **Soft Refresh**: Default sync period set by `appResyncPeriod` (statusRefreshTimeout)\n  - Every reconciliation cycle checks: `app.Status.ReconciledAt == nil || app.Status.ReconciledAt.Add(statusRefreshTimeout).Before(time.Now().UTC())`\n  - Location: `/workspace/controller/appcontroller.go:1892`\n\n- **Hard Refresh**: Periodic full reconciliation set by `appHardResyncPeriod` (statusHardRefreshTimeout)\n  - Triggers complete re-comparison when: `app.Status.ReconciledAt.Add(statusHardRefreshTimeout).Before(time.Now().UTC())`\n  - Location: `/workspace/controller/appcontroller.go:1893`\n\n**B. Manual Sync (User-triggered)**\n- Detected by: `if requestedType, ok := app.IsRefreshRequested(); ok`\n- User can request Normal or Hard refresh via Application status\n- Location: `/workspace/controller/appcontroller.go:1895`\n\n**D. Related Resource Changes (Cluster Drift Detection)**\n- Live state cache detects changes to managed resources and calls `handleObjectUpdated()`\n- For managed resources: triggers `CompareWithRecent` level refresh\n- For orphaned resources: triggers full comparison\n- Location: `/workspace/controller/appcontroller.go:419-479`\n\n---\n\n### 2. **Method That Detects Drift and Enqueues Refresh**\n\n**Primary Method**: `needRefreshAppStatus()` (lines 1886-1937)\n```go\nfunc (ctrl *ApplicationController) needRefreshAppStatus(app *appv1.Application, \n    statusRefreshTimeout, statusHardRefreshTimeout time.Duration) \n    (bool, appv1.RefreshType, CompareWith)\n```\n\n**Detection Logic**:\n- Checks if `ReconciledAt` is past soft/hard refresh timeout\n- Checks for spec changes (source, destination, ignoreDifferences)\n- Checks for user-requested refresh\n- Returns: `(needRefresh bool, refreshType RefreshType, compareWith CompareWith)`\n\n**Enqueue Method**: `requestAppRefresh()` (lines 943-960)\n```go\nfunc (ctrl *ApplicationController) requestAppRefresh(appName string, \n    compareWith *CompareWith, after *time.Duration)\n```\n\nThis method:\n- Stores refresh request in `ctrl.refreshRequestedApps[appName]`\n- Adds app to `appRefreshQueue` with optional rate limiting or delay\n- Location: `/workspace/controller/appcontroller.go:943-960`\n\n**Processing Queue**: `processAppRefreshQueueItem()` (lines 1598-1640)\n- Gets apps from `ctrl.appRefreshQueue`\n- Calls `needRefreshAppStatus()` to determine if refresh needed\n- Proceeds with manifest generation if needed\n- Location: `/workspace/controller/appcontroller.go:1598`\n\n---\n\n### 3. **ApplicationController to RepoServer Communication (gRPC)**\n\n**Method**: `GenerateManifest()` RPC call\n\n**Signature** (from proto):\n```protobuf\nservice RepoServerService {\n    rpc GenerateManifest(ManifestRequest) returns (ManifestResponse) {}\n}\n```\n\n**Request Flow**:\n1. ApplicationController calls `GetRepoObjs()` in `appStateManager`\n2. `GetRepoObjs()` creates gRPC connection:\n   ```go\n   conn, repoClient, err := m.repoClientset.NewRepoServerClient()\n   ```\n3. Makes synchronous gRPC call:\n   ```go\n   manifestInfo, err := repoClient.GenerateManifest(context.Background(), &apiclient.ManifestRequest{...})\n   ```\n   Location: `/workspace/controller/state.go:269`\n\n**Response Type**: `ManifestResponse` containing:\n- Generated manifests (as YAML strings)\n- Resolved revision (commit SHA)\n- Source type detection\n- Git signature verification results\n\nLocation: `/workspace/reposerver/repository/repository.proto:92-103`\n\n---\n\n### 4. **ManifestRequest Data Structure**\n\nThe request contains comprehensive application and build context:\n\n**Core Repository Info**:\n- `Repo`: Repository credentials and URL\n- `Revision`: Git revision/branch/tag\n- `AppName`: Application qualified name\n- `Namespace`: Target namespace\n\n**Application Source Details**:\n- `ApplicationSource`: Kustomize/Helm/Plugin configuration\n- `Path`: Path within repository\n- `Chart`: Helm chart name (if Helm source)\n- `KustomizeOptions`: Kustomize-specific settings\n\n**Build Environment**:\n- `KubeVersion`: Target cluster Kubernetes version\n- `ApiVersions`: Supported API versions from cluster\n- `HelmOptions`: Helm rendering options\n- `EnabledSourceTypes`: Plugin/CMP availability\n- `KustomizeOptions`: Kustomize build options\n\n**Request Modifiers**:\n- `NoCache`: Bypass manifest cache\n- `NoRevisionCache`: Force git revision resolution\n- `VerifySignature`: GPG signature verification\n- `TrackingMethod`: Resource tracking approach\n\n**Multi-source Support**:\n- `HasMultipleSources`: Boolean flag\n- `RefSources`: Referenced source revisions map\n- `Repos`: All permitted repositories\n- `HelmRepoCreds`: Helm repository credentials\n\n**Project & Security**:\n- `ProjectName`: Application project\n- `ProjectSourceRepos`: Permitted source repos\n- `AnnotationManifestGeneratePaths`: Optimization hint\n- `InstallationID`: Argo CD instance identifier\n\nFull definition: `/workspace/reposerver/repository/repository.proto:10-45`\n\n**Implementation in Controller**: `/workspace/controller/state.go:269-293`\n\n---\n\n### 5. **How RepoServer Fetches and Caches Git Repository**\n\n**Git Repository Caching Architecture**:\n\n**A. Cache Layer** (3 levels):\n1. **Revision Cache** (in-memory)\n   - Caches resolved commit SHAs for branches/tags\n   - TTL: configurable (default 3 minutes)\n   - Lock timeout: prevents duplicate requests\n   - Location: `/workspace/reposerver/cache/cache.go`\n\n2. **Manifest Cache**\n   - Stores generated manifests by source configuration hash\n   - Prevents redundant manifest generation\n   - Can be bypassed with `NoCache` flag\n\n3. **Git Repository Cache** (disk-based)\n   - Each repository gets randomized temp directory path\n   - Path initialized: `s.gitRepoPaths.GetPath(git.NormalizeGitURL(repo.Repo))`\n   - Location: `/workspace/reposerver/repository/repository.go:2409`\n\n**B. Repository Initialization Process**:\n\n1. **Client Creation**: `newClient()` (lines 2408-2415)\n   ```go\n   func (s *Service) newClient(repo *v1alpha1.Repository, opts ...git.ClientOpts) \n       (git.Client, error)\n   ```\n   - Gets or creates randomized repo path\n   - Applies git credentials from secret store\n   - Registers metrics event handlers\n\n2. **Revision Resolution**: `newClientResolveRevision()` (lines 2419-2430)\n   ```go\n   func (s *Service) newClientResolveRevision(repo *v1alpha1.Repository, revision string, \n       opts ...git.ClientOpts) (git.Client, string, error)\n   ```\n   - Creates git client\n   - Calls `gitClient.LsRemote(revision)` to resolve to commit SHA\n   - Returns (client, commitSHA, error)\n\n3. **Cache Option**: Git client supports caching via `git.WithCache()` option\n   - Caches revision resolution results\n   - Prevents redundant `git ls-remote` calls\n   - Location: `/workspace/reposerver/repository/repository.go:309`\n\n**C. Manifest Generation Flow**:\n\n1. **Pre-fetch Cache Check** (lines 532-536):\n   ```go\n   cacheFn := func(cacheKey string, refSourceCommitSHAs cache.ResolvedRevisions, \n       firstInvocation bool) (bool, error)\n   ```\n   - Early return if manifest found in cache\n\n2. **Repository Operation** (lines 289-454):\n   ```go\n   func (s *Service) runRepoOperation(ctx context.Context, revision string, \n       repo *v1alpha1.Repository, source *v1alpha1.ApplicationSource, ...)\n   ```\n   - Resolves revision to commit SHA\n   - Acquires semaphore lock (parallelism control)\n   - For Git sources: Calls `checkoutRevision()` which:\n     - Initializes git repository at cached path\n     - Runs `git fetch` to get latest refs\n     - Runs `git checkout <commit>` to checkout specific revision\n     - Applies submodule handling if enabled\n   - For Helm: Downloads and extracts chart\n\n3. **Lock-based Concurrency Control** (line 383):\n   ```go\n   closer, err := s.repoLock.Lock(gitClient.Root(), revision, \n       settings.allowConcurrent, func() (goio.Closer, error) {\n       return s.checkoutRevision(gitClient, revision, submoduleEnabled)\n   })\n   ```\n   - Repository-level lock prevents duplicate fetches\n   - Concurrent processing controlled by semaphore\n\n**D. Checkout Process**: `checkoutRevision()` (lines 2495+)\n- Initializes directory permissions (0o700)\n- Performs git operations in locked directory\n- Removes permissions after completion (0o000)\n- Returns checkout status\n\n**E. Post-generation Cache Update** (lines 831-874):\n```go\nok, err := s.getManifestCacheEntry(cacheKey, q, refSourceCommitSHAs, firstInvocation)\nif ok {\n    return cached manifest\n}\n// Generate...\nerr = s.cache.SetManifests(cacheKey, appSourceCopy, q.RefSources, q, ...)\n```\n\n**Key Caching Features**:\n- **Credential-secure**: Credentials never cached, only repo URLs\n- **Multi-level**: Revision → Manifest → Output\n- **Lock-based**: Prevents parallel git operations on same repo\n- **Configurable TTL**: Repo cache (24h default), Revision cache (3min default)\n- **Bypass options**: `NoCache` and `NoRevisionCache` flags\n\n---\n\n### **Summary Flow Diagram**\n\n```\nEvent Triggers:\n├── Time-based: statusRefreshTimeout / statusHardRefreshTimeout\n├── Resource Change: Application spec modified\n├── Manual Sync: User-requested refresh\n└── Drift Detection: Related resource changes\n\n    ↓\nneedRefreshAppStatus() \n├─ Determines need for refresh\n└─ Returns (needRefresh, refreshType, comparisonLevel)\n\n    ↓\nprocessAppRefreshQueueItem()\n├─ Gets app from refresh queue\n└─ Calls GetRepoObjs() for each source\n\n    ↓\nGetRepoObjs() → repoClient.GenerateManifest(ManifestRequest)\n    │\n    ├─ Creates gRPC connection\n    └─ Sends ManifestRequest with:\n       ├─ Repository credentials\n       ├─ Git revision\n       ├─ Application source config\n       └─ Build environment info\n\n    ↓\nRepoServer GenerateManifest():\n├─ Caches revision: git ls-remote\n├─ Checks manifest cache (hit → return)\n├─ Runs runRepoOperation():\n│  ├─ newClientResolveRevision() → (gitClient, commitSHA)\n│  ├─ acquires repoLock\n│  ├─ checkoutRevision():\n│  │  ├─ git fetch\n│  │  └─ git checkout <commit>\n│  └─ Generates manifests Related Resource Changes (Cluster Drift Detection)**\n- Live state cache detects changes to managed resources and calls `handleObjectUpdated()`\n- For managed resources: triggers `CompareWithRecent` level refresh\n- For orphaned resources: triggers full comparison\n- Location: `/workspace/controller/appcontroller.go:419-479`\n\n---\n\n### 2. **Method That Detects Drift and Enqueues Refresh**\n\n**Primary Method**: `needRefreshAppStatus()` (lines 1886-1937)\n```go\nfunc (ctrl *ApplicationController) needRefreshAppStatus(app *appv1.Application, \n    statusRefreshTimeout, statusHardRefreshTimeout time.Duration) \n    (bool, appv1.RefreshType, CompareWith)\n```\n\n**Detection Logic**:\n- Checks if `ReconciledAt` is past soft/hard refresh timeout\n- Checks for spec changes (source, destination, ignoreDifferences)\n- Checks for user-requested refresh\n- Returns: `(needRefresh bool, refreshType RefreshType, compareWith CompareWith)`\n\n**Enqueue Method**: `requestAppRefresh()` (lines 943-960)\n```go\nfunc (ctrl *ApplicationController) requestAppRefresh(appName string, \n    compareWith *CompareWith, after *time.Duration)\n```\n\nThis method:\n- Stores refresh request in `ctrl.refreshRequestedApps[appName]`\n- Adds app to `appRefreshQueue` with optional rate limiting or delay\n- Location: `/workspace/controller/appcontroller.go:943-960`\n\n**Processing Queue**: `processAppRefreshQueueItem()` (lines 1598-1640)\n- Gets apps from `ctrl.appRefreshQueue`\n- Calls `needRefreshAppStatus()` to determine if refresh needed\n- Proceeds with manifest generation if needed\n- Location: `/workspace/controller/appcontroller.go:1598`\n\n---\n\n### 3. **ApplicationController to RepoServer Communication (gRPC)**\n\n**Method**: `GenerateManifest()` RPC call\n\n**Signature** (from proto):\n```protobuf\nservice RepoServerService {\n    rpc GenerateManifest(ManifestRequest) returns (ManifestResponse) {}\n}\n```\n\n**Request Flow**:\n1. ApplicationController calls `GetRepoObjs()` in `appStateManager`\n2. `GetRepoObjs()` creates gRPC connection:\n   ```go\n   conn, repoClient, err := m.repoClientset.NewRepoServerClient()\n   ```\n3. Makes synchronous gRPC call:\n   ```go\n   manifestInfo, err := repoClient.GenerateManifest(context.Background(), &apiclient.ManifestRequest{...})\n   ```\n   Location: `/workspace/controller/state.go:269`\n\n**Response Type**: `ManifestResponse` containing:\n- Generated manifests (as YAML strings)\n- Resolved revision (commit SHA)\n- Source type detection\n- Git signature verification results\n\nLocation: `/workspace/reposerver/repository/repository.proto:92-103`\n\n---\n\n### 4. **ManifestRequest Data Structure**\n\nThe request contains comprehensive application and build context:\n\n**Core Repository Info**:\n- `Repo`: Repository credentials and URL\n- `Revision`: Git revision/branch/tag\n- `AppName`: Application qualified name\n- `Namespace`: Target namespace\n\n**Application Source Details**:\n- `ApplicationSource`: Kustomize/Helm/Plugin configuration\n- `Path`: Path within repository\n- `Chart`: Helm chart name (if Helm source)\n- `KustomizeOptions`: Kustomize-specific settings\n\n**Build Environment**:\n- `KubeVersion`: Target cluster Kubernetes version\n- `ApiVersions`: Supported API versions from cluster\n- `HelmOptions`: Helm rendering options\n- `EnabledSourceTypes`: Plugin/CMP availability\n- `KustomizeOptions`: Kustomize build options\n\n**Request Modifiers**:\n- `NoCache`: Bypass manifest cache\n- `NoRevisionCache`: Force git revision resolution\n- `VerifySignature`: GPG signature verification\n- `TrackingMethod`: Resource tracking approach\n\n**Multi-source Support**:\n- `HasMultipleSources`: Boolean flag\n- `RefSources`: Referenced source revisions map\n- `Repos`: All permitted repositories\n- `HelmRepoCreds`: Helm repository credentials\n\n**Project & Security**:\n- `ProjectName`: Application project\n- `ProjectSourceRepos`: Permitted source repos\n- `AnnotationManifestGeneratePaths`: Optimization hint\n- `InstallationID`: Argo CD instance identifier\n\nFull definition: `/workspace/reposerver/repository/repository.proto:10-45`\n\n**Implementation in Controller**: `/workspace/controller/state.go:269-293`\n\n---\n\n### 5. **How RepoServer Fetches and Caches Git Repository**\n\n**Git Repository Caching Architecture**:\n\n**A. Cache Layer** (3 levels):\n1. **Revision Cache** (in-memory)\n   - Caches resolved commit SHAs for branches/tags\n   - TTL: configurable (default 3 minutes)\n   - Lock timeout: prevents duplicate requests\n   - Location: `/workspace/reposerver/cache/cache.go`\n\n2. **Manifest Cache**\n   - Stores generated manifests by source configuration hash\n   - Prevents redundant manifest generation\n   - Can be bypassed with `NoCache` flag\n\n3. **Git Repository Cache** (disk-based)\n   - Each repository gets randomized temp directory path\n   - Path initialized: `s.gitRepoPaths.GetPath(git.NormalizeGitURL(repo.Repo))`\n   - Location: `/workspace/reposerver/repository/repository.go:2409`\n\n**B. Repository Initialization Process**:\n\n1. **Client Creation**: `newClient()` (lines 2408-2415)\n   ```go\n   func (s *Service) newClient(repo *v1alpha1.Repository, opts ...git.ClientOpts) \n       (git.Client, error)\n   ```\n   - Gets or creates randomized repo path\n   - Applies git credentials from secret store\n   - Registers metrics event handlers\n\n2. **Revision Resolution**: `newClientResolveRevision()` (lines 2419-2430)\n   ```go\n   func (s *Service) newClientResolveRevision(repo *v1alpha1.Repository, revision string, \n       opts ...git.ClientOpts) (git.Client, string, error)\n   ```\n   - Creates git client\n   - Calls `gitClient.LsRemote(revision)` to resolve to commit SHA\n   - Returns (client, commitSHA, error)\n\n3. **Cache Option**: Git client supports caching via `git.WithCache()` option\n   - Caches revision resolution results\n   - Prevents redundant `git ls-remote` calls\n   - Location: `/workspace/reposerver/repository/repository.go:309`\n\n**C. Manifest Generation Flow**:\n\n1. **Pre-fetch Cache Check** (lines 532-536):\n   ```go\n   cacheFn := func(cacheKey string, refSourceCommitSHAs cache.ResolvedRevisions, \n       firstInvocation bool) (bool, error)\n   ```\n   - Early return if manifest found in cache\n\n2. **Repository Operation** (lines 289-454):\n   ```go\n   func (s *Service) runRepoOperation(ctx context.Context, revision string, \n       repo *v1alpha1.Repository, source *v1alpha1.ApplicationSource, ...)\n   ```\n   - Resolves revision to commit SHA\n   - Acquires semaphore lock (parallelism control)\n   - For Git sources: Calls `checkoutRevision()` which:\n     - Initializes git repository at cached path\n     - Runs `git fetch` to get latest refs\n     - Runs `git checkout <commit>` to checkout specific revision\n     - Applies submodule handling if enabled\n   - For Helm: Downloads and extracts chart\n\n3. **Lock-based Concurrency Control** (line 383):\n   ```go\n   closer, err := s.repoLock.Lock(gitClient.Root(), revision, \n       settings.allowConcurrent, func() (goio.Closer, error) {\n       return s.checkoutRevision(gitClient, revision, submoduleEnabled)\n   })\n   ```\n   - Repository-level lock prevents duplicate fetches\n   - Concurrent processing controlled by semaphore\n\n**D. Checkout Process**: `checkoutRevision()` (lines 2495+)\n- Initializes directory permissions (0o700)\n- Performs git operations in locked directory\n- Removes permissions after completion (0o000)\n- Returns checkout status\n\n**E. Post-generation Cache Update** (lines 831-874):\n```go\nok, err := s.getManifestCacheEntry(cacheKey, q, refSourceCommitSHAs, firstInvocation)\nif ok {\n    return cached manifest\n}\n// Generate...\nerr = s.cache.SetManifests(cacheKey, appSourceCopy, q.RefSources, q, ...)\n```\n\n**Key Caching Features**:\n- **Credential-secure**: Credentials never cached, only repo URLs\n- **Multi-level**: Revision → Manifest → Output\n- **Lock-based**: Prevents parallel git operations on same repo\n- **Configurable TTL**: Repo cache (24h default), Revision cache (3min default)\n- **Bypass options**: `NoCache` and `NoRevisionCache` flags\n\n---\n\n### **Summary Flow Diagram**\n\n```\nEvent Triggers:\n├── Time-based: statusRefreshTimeout / statusHardRefreshTimeout\n├── Resource Change: Application spec modified\n├── Manual Sync: User-requested refresh\n└── Drift Detection: Related resource changes\n\n    ↓\nneedRefreshAppStatus() \n├─ Determines need for refresh\n└─ Returns (needRefresh, refreshType, comparisonLevel)\n\n    ↓\nprocessAppRefreshQueueItem()\n├─ Gets app from refresh queue\n└─ Calls GetRepoObjs() for each source\n\n    ↓\nGetRepoObjs() → repoClient.GenerateManifest(ManifestRequest)\n    │\n    ├─ Creates gRPC connection\n    └─ Sends ManifestRequest with:\n       ├─ Repository credentials\n       ├─ Git revision\n       ├─ Application source config\n       └─ Build environment info\n\n    ↓\nRepoServer GenerateManifest():\n├─ Caches revision: git ls-remote\n├─ Checks manifest cache (hit → return)\n├─ Runs runRepoOperation():\n│  ├─ newClientResolveRevision() → (gitClient, commitSHA)\n│  ├─ acquires repoLock\n│  ├─ checkoutRevision():\n│  │  ├─ git fetch\n│  │  └─ git checkout <commit>\n│  └─ Generates manifests (Kustomize/Helm/Plugins)\n└─ Caches result → returns ManifestResponse\n```\n\n---\n\n### **Key Files**\n\n- **Controller Reconciliation**: `/workspace/controller/appcontroller.go` (lines 1598-1937)\n- 1583→\t\tif err != nil {\n  1584→\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%s: %w\", filename, err)\n  1585→\t\t}\n  1586→\t\tpatch, err := os.ReadFile(filename)\n  1587→\t\tif err != nil {\n  1588→\t\t\treturn If `source.Helm != nil` → Helm type\n  - If `source.Kustomize != nil` → Kustomize type  \n  - If `source.Directory != nil` → Directory type\n  - If `source.Plugin != nil` → Plugin type\n  - If multiple types are set → Error\n  - If none are set → Falls through to discovery\n\n**Phase 2: Automatic Discovery (if explicit type is not set)**\n- `discovery.AppType()` / `discovery.Discover()` checks:\n  1. **Config Management Plugin (CMP)**: Calls `DetectConfigManagementPlugin()` first\n     - Checks `common.GetPluginSockFilePath()` for available plugins\n     - Each plugin is queried via `cmpSupports()` to see if it matches the repository\n     - If a plugin is named in ApplicationSource, it's forced to be used\n  2. **Helm**: Looks for `Chart.yaml` in the directory\n  3. **Kustomize**: Looks for Kustomization files (`kustomization.yaml`, `kustomization.yml`, `Kustomization`)\n  4. **Directory**: Default fallback for plain YAML/JSON files\n\nLocation: `/workspace/util/app/discovery/discovery.go` (lines 36-82)\n\n**IsHelm Detection:**\n```go\nfunc (source *ApplicationSource) IsHelm() bool {\n    return source.Chart != \"\"\n}\n```\n\n### 2. **Sequence of Operations for Manifest Generation**\n\n```\nGenerateManifest (lines 518-586)\n    ↓\n    cacheFn setup → getManifestCacheEntry (checks cache first)\n    ↓\n    runRepoOperation (lines 289-454)\n        ├─ For Helm: Resolve helm client & extract chart\n        └─ For Git: Clone/checkout repo to revision\n    ↓\n    runManifestGen (creates channels for async operation)\n    ↓\n    runManifestGenAsync (lines 704-812)\n        ├─ Handle multi-source references (RefSources)\n        ├─ Call GenerateManifests()\n        ├─ Handle cache update/error caching\n        └─ Send response through channels\n    ↓\n    GenerateManifests (lines 1421-1522)\n        ├─ GetAppSourceType() - If errors exceed `PauseGenerationAfterFailedGenerationAttempts` threshold:\n     - Returns cached error for `PauseGenerationOnFailureForMinutes`\n     - OR after `PauseGenerationOnFailureForRequests` cached responses returned\n     - Then resets and retries\n5. **Source Changes**: Cache key includes ref source commit SHAs, so any referenced source changes invalidate cache\n6. **Configuration Changes**: Any change to ApplicationSource, KubeVersion, APIVersions, tracking method, or app label key invalidates cache\n\n**Expiration**:\n- `repoCacheExpiration`: Default 24 hours (ARGOCD_REPO_CACHE_EXPIRATION)\n- `revisionCacheExpiration`: Default 3 minutes (ARGOCD_RECONCILIATION_TIMEOUT)\n- `revisionCacheLockTimeout`: Default 10 seconds (ARGOCD_REVISION_CACHE_LOCK_TIMEOUT)\n\n### 4. **ManifestResponse Contents**\n\nLocation: `/workspace/reposerver/repository/repository.proto` (lines 92-103)\n\n```protobuf\nmessage ManifestResponse {\n    repeated string manifests = 1;           // Generated manifests as JSON strings\n    string namespace = 2;                    // Target namespace\n    string server = 3;                       // API server (empty in generation)\n    string revision = 4;                     // Resolved git commit SHA\n    string sourceType = 6;                   // \"Helm\", \"Kustomize\", \"Directory\", or \"Plugin\"\n    string verifyResult = 7;                 // Git verify-commit output (empty for Helm)\n    repeated string commands = 8;            // Commands used to hydrate manifests\n}\n```\n\n**Content Details**:\n- **manifests**: Array of JSON-encoded Kubernetes resource objects with tracking labels applied\n- **sourceType**: String representation of ApplicationSourceType (Helm, Kustomize, Plugin, Directory)\n- **revision**: The actual commit SHA (returned even for Helm/charts)\n- **commands**: List of shell commands executed (e.g., `helm template ...`, `kustomize build ...`)\n- **namespace**: Target namespace from ApplicationSource or default\n- **verifyResult**: Non-empty only for signed Git commits\n\n### Key Files Involved:\n\n1. `/workspace/reposerver/repository/repository.go` - 92→\trevisionUpdated    bool\n    93→}\n    94→\n    95→func (res *comparisonResult) GetSyncStatus() *v1alpha1.SyncStatus {\n    96→\treturn res.syncStatus\n    97→}\n    98→\n    99→func (res   355→\n   356→func unmarshalManifests(manifests []string) ([]*unstructured.Unstructured, error) {\n   357→\ttargetObjs := make([]*unstructured.Unstructured, 0)\n   358→\tfor _, manifest := range manifests {\n   359→\t\tobj, err := v1alpha1.UnmarshalToUnstructured(manifest)\n   360→\t\tif err != nil {\n   361→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   362→\t\t}\n   363→\t\ttargetObjs = append(targetObjs, 371→\tinfoProvider kubeutil.ResourceInfoProvider,\n   372→) ([]*unstructured.Unstructured, []v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition, error) {\n   373→\ttargetByKey := make(map[kubeutil.ResourceKey][]*unstructured.Unstructured)\n   374→\tfor i := range objs {\n   375→\t\tobj := objs[i]\n   376→\t\tif obj == nil {\n   377→\t\t\tcontinue\n   378→\t\t}\n   379→\t\tisNamespaced := kubeutil.IsNamespacedOrUnknown(infoProvider, obj.GroupVersionKind().GroupKind())\n   380→\t\tif !isNamespaced {\n   381→\t\t\tobj.SetNamespace(\"\")\n   382→\t\t} else if obj.GetNamespace() == \"\" {\n   383→\t\t\tobj.SetNamespace(namespace)\n   384→\t\t}\n   385→\t\tkey := kubeutil.GetResourceKey(obj)\n   386→\t\tif key.Name == \"\" && obj.GetGenerateName() != \"\" {\n   387→\t\t\tkey.Name = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s%d\", obj.GetGenerateName(), i)\n   388→\t\t}\n   389→\t\ttargetByKey[key] = append(targetByKey[key], obj)\n   390→\t}\n   391→\tconditions := make([]v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition, 0)\n   392→\tresult := make([]*unstructured.Unstructured, 0)\n   393→\tfor key, targets := range targetByKey {\n   394→\t\tif len(targets) > 1 {\n   395→\t\t\tnow := metav1.Now()\n   396→\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{\n   397→\t\t\t\tType:               v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionRepeatedResourceWarning,\n   398→\t\t\t\tMessage:            fmt.Sprintf(\"Resource %s appeared %d times among application resources.\", key.String(), len(targets)),\n   399→\t\t\t\tLastTransitionTime: &now,\n   400→\t\t\t})\n   401→\t\t}\n   402→\t\tresult = append(result, targets[len(targets)-1])\n   403→\t}\n   404→\n   405→\treturn result, conditions, nil\n   406→}\n   407→\n   408→// getComparisonSettings will return the system level settings related to the\n   409→// diff/normalization process.\n   410→func (m *appStateManager) getComparisonSettings() (string, map[string]v1alpha1.ResourceOverride, *settings.ResourcesFilter, string, error) {\n   411→\tresourceOverrides, err := m.settingsMgr.GetResourceOverrides()\n   412→\tif err != nil {\n   413→\t\treturn \"\", nil, nil, \"\", err\n   414→\t}\n   415→\tappLabelKey, err := m.settingsMgr.GetAppInstanceLabelKey()\n   416→\tif err != nil {\n   417→\t\treturn \"\", nil, nil, \"\", err\n   418→\t}\n   419→\tresFilter, err := m.settingsMgr.GetResourcesFilter()\n   420→\tif err != nil {\n   421→\t\treturn \"\", nil, nil, \"\", err\n   422→\t}\n   423→\tinstallationID, err := m.settingsMgr.GetInstallationID()\n   424→\tif err != nil {\n   425→\t\treturn \"\", nil, nil, \"\", err\n   426→\t}\n   427→\treturn appLabelKey, resourceOverrides, resFilter, installationID, nil\n   428→}\n   429→\n   430→// verifyGnuPGSignature verifies the result of a GnuPG operation for a given git\n   431→// revision.\n   432→func verifyGnuPGSignature(revision string, project *v1alpha1.AppProject, manifestInfo *apiclient.ManifestResponse) []v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition {\n   433→\tnow := metav1.Now()\n   434→\tconditions := make([]v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition, 0)\n   435→\t// We need to have some data in the verification result to parse, otherwise there was no signature\n   436→\tif manifestInfo.VerifyResult != \"\" {\n   437→\t\tverifyResult := gpg.ParseGitCommitVerification(manifestInfo.VerifyResult)\n   438→\t\tswitch verifyResult.Result {\n   439→\t\tcase gpg.VerifyResultGood:\n   440→\t\t\t// This is the only case we allow to sync to, but we need to make sure signing key is allowed\n   441→\t\t\tvalidKey := false\n   442→\t\t\tfor _, k := range project.Spec.SignatureKeys {\n   443→\t\t\t\tif gpg.KeyID(k.KeyID) == gpg.KeyID(verifyResult.KeyID) && gpg.KeyID(k.KeyID) != \"\" {\n   444→\t\t\t\t\tvalidKey = true\n   445→\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n   446→\t\t\t\t}\n   447→\t\t\t}\n   448→\t\t\tif !validKey {\n   449→\t\t\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"Found good signature made with %s key %s, but this key is not allowed in AppProject\",\n   450→\t\t\t\t\tverifyResult.Cipher, verifyResult.KeyID)\n   451→\t\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   452→\t\t\t}\n   453→\t\tcase gpg.VerifyResultInvalid:\n   454→\t\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"Found signature made with %s key %s, but verification result was invalid: '%s'\",\n   455→\t\t\t\tverifyResult.Cipher, verifyResult.KeyID, verifyResult.Message)\n   456→\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   457→\t\tdefault:\n   458→\t\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"Could not verify commit signature on revision '%s', check logs for more information.\", revision)\n   459→\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   460→\t\t}\n   461→\t} else {\n   462→\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"Target revision %s in Git is not signed, but a signature is required\", revision)\n   463→\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   464→\t}\n   465→\n   466→\treturn conditions\n   467→}\n   468→\n   469→func isManagedNamespace(ns *unstructured.Unstructured, app *v1alpha1.Application) bool {\n   470→\treturn ns != nil && ns.GetKind() == kubeutil.NamespaceKind && ns.GetName() == app.Spec.Destination.Namespace && app.Spec.SyncPolicy != nil && app.Spec.SyncPolicy.ManagedNamespaceMetadata != nil\n   471→}\n   472→\n   473→// CompareAppState compares application git state to the live app state, using the specified\n   474→// revision and supplied source. If revision or overrides are empty, then compares against\n   475→// revision and overrides in the app spec.\n   476→func (m *appStateManager) CompareAppState(app *v1alpha1.Application, project *v1alpha1.AppProject, revisions []string, sources []v1alpha1.ApplicationSource, noCache bool, noRevisionCache bool, localManifests []string, hasMultipleSources bool, rollback bool) (*comparisonResult, error) {\n   477→\tts := stats.NewTimingStats()\n   478→\tappLabelKey, resourceOverrides, resFilter, installationID, err := m.getComparisonSettings()\n   479→\n   480→\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"settings_ms\")\n   481→\n   482→\t// return unknown comparison result if basic comparison settings cannot be loaded\n   483→\tif err != nil {\n   484→\t\tif hasMultipleSources {\n   485→\t\t\treturn &comparisonResult{\n   486→\t\t\t\tsyncStatus: &v1alpha1.SyncStatus{\n   487→\t\t\t\t\tComparedTo: app.Spec.BuildComparedToStatus(),\n   488→\t\t\t\t\tStatus:     v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeUnknown,\n   489→\t\t\t\t\tRevisions:  revisions,\n   490→\t\t\t\t},\n   491→\t\t\t\thealthStatus: &v1alpha1.HealthStatus{Status: health.HealthStatusUnknown},\n   492→\t\t\t}, nil\n   493→\t\t} else {\n   494→\t\t\treturn &comparisonResult{\n   495→\t\t\t\tsyncStatus: &v1alpha1.SyncStatus{\n   496→\t\t\t\t\tComparedTo: app.Spec.BuildComparedToStatus(),\n   497→\t\t\t\t\tStatus:     v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeUnknown,\n   498→\t\t\t\t\tRevision:   revisions[0],\n   499→\t\t\t\t},\n   500→\t\t\t\thealthStatus: &v1alpha1.HealthStatus{Status: health.HealthStatusUnknown},\n   501→\t\t\t}, nil\n   502→\t\t}\n   503→\t}\n   504→\n   505→\t// When signature keys are defined in the project spec, we need to verify the signature on the Git revision\n   506→\tverifySignature := false\n   507→\tif len(project.Spec.SignatureKeys) > 0 && gpg.IsGPGEnabled() {\n   508→\t\tverifySignature = true\n   509→\t}\n   510→\n   511→\t// do best effort loading live and target state to present as much information about app state as possible\n   512→\tfailedToLoadObjs := false\n   513→\tconditions := make([]v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition, 0)\n   514→\n   515→\tlogCtx := log.WithField(\"application\", app.QualifiedName())\n   516→\tlogCtx.Infof(\"Comparing app state (cluster: %s, namespace: %s)\", app.Spec.Destination.Server, app.Spec.Destination.Namespace)\n   517→\n   518→\tvar targetObjs []*unstructured.Unstructured\n   519→\tnow := metav1.Now()\n   520→\n   521→\tvar manifestInfos []*apiclient.ManifestResponse\n   522→\ttargetNsExists := false\n   523→\n   524→\tvar revisionUpdated bool\n   525→\n   526→\tif len(localManifests) == 0 {\n   527→\t\t// If the length of revisions is not same as the length of sources,\n   528→\t\t// we take the revisions from the sources directly for all the sources.\n   529→\t\tif len(revisions) != len(sources) {\n   530→\t\t\trevisions = make([]string, 0)\n   531→\t\t\tfor _, source := range sources {\n   532→\t\t\t\trevisions = append(revisions, source.TargetRevision)\n   533→\t\t\t}\n   534→\t\t}\n   535→\n   536→\t\ttargetObjs, manifestInfos, revisionUpdated, err = m.GetRepoObjs(app, sources, appLabelKey, revisions, noCache, noRevisionCache, verifySignature, project, rollback, true)\n   537→\t\tif err != nil {\n   538→\t\t\ttargetObjs = make([]*unstructured.Unstructured, 0)\n   539→\t\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to load target state: %s\", err.Error())\n   540→\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   541→\t\t\tif firstSeen, ok := m.repoErrorCache.Load(app.Name); ok {\n   542→\t\t\t\tif time.Since(firstSeen.(time.Time)) <= m.repoErrorGracePeriod && !noRevisionCache {\n   543→\t\t\t\t\t// if first seen is less than grace period and it's not a Level 3 comparison,\n   544→\t\t\t\t\t// ignore error and short circuit\n   545→\t\t\t\t\tlogCtx.Debugf(\"Ignoring repo error %v, already encountered error in grace period\", err.Error())\n   546→\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, CompareStateRepoError\n   547→\t\t\t\t}\n   548→\t\t\t} else if !noRevisionCache {\n   549→\t\t\t\tlogCtx.Debugf(\"Ignoring repo error %v, new occurrence\", err.Error())\n   550→\t\t\t\tm.repoErrorCache.Store(app.Name, time.Now())\n   551→\t\t\t\treturn nil, CompareStateRepoError\n   552→\t\t\t}\n   553→\t\t\tfailedToLoadObjs = true\n   554→\t\t} else {\n   555→\t\t\tm.repoErrorCache.Delete(app.Name)\n   556→\t\t}\n   557→\t} else {\n   558→\t\t// Prevent applying local manifests for now when signature verification is enabled\n   559→\t\t// This is also enforced on API level, but as a last resort, we also enforce it here\n   560→\t\tif gpg.IsGPGEnabled() && verifySignature {\n   561→\t\t\tmsg := \"Cannot use local manifests when signature verification is required\"\n   562→\t\t\ttargetObjs = make([]*unstructured.Unstructured, 0)\n   563→\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   564→\t\t\tfailedToLoadObjs = true\n   565→\t\t} else {\n   566→\t\t\ttargetObjs, err = unmarshalManifests(localManifests)\n   567→\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   568→\t\t\t\ttargetObjs = make([]*unstructured.Unstructured, 0)\n   569→\t\t\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to load local manifests: %s\", err.Error())\n   570→\t\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   571→\t\t\t\tfailedToLoadObjs = true\n   572→\t\t\t}\n   573→\t\t}\n   574→\t\t// empty out manifestInfoMap\n   575→\t\tmanifestInfos = make([]*apiclient.ManifestResponse, 0)\n   576→\t}\n   577→\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"git_ms\")\n   578→\n   579→\tvar infoProvider kubeutil.ResourceInfoProvider\n   580→\tinfoProvider, err = m.liveStateCache.GetClusterCache(app.Spec.Destination.Server)\n   581→\tif err != nil {\n   582→\t\tinfoProvider = &resourceInfoProviderStub{}\n   583→\t}\n   584→\ttargetObjs, dedupConditions, err := DeduplicateTargetObjects(app.Spec.Destination.Namespace, targetObjs, infoProvider)\n   585→\tif err != nil {\n   586→\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to deduplicate target state: %s\", err.Error())\n   587→\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   588→\t}\n   589→\tconditions = append(conditions, dedupConditions...)\n   590→\tfor i := len(targetObjs) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {\n   591→\t\ttargetObj := targetObjs[i]\n   592→\t\tgvk := targetObj.GroupVersionKind()\n   593→\t\tif resFilter.IsExcludedResource(gvk.Group, gvk.Kind, app.Spec.Destination.Server) {\n   594→\t\t\ttargetObjs = append(targetObjs[:i], targetObjs[i+1:]...)\n   595→\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{\n   596→\t\t\t\tType:               v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionExcludedResourceWarning,\n   597→\t\t\t\tMessage:            fmt.Sprintf(\"Resource %s/%s %s is excluded in the settings\", gvk.Group, gvk.Kind, targetObj.GetName()),\n   598→\t\t\t\tLastTransitionTime: &now,\n   599→\t\t\t})\n   600→\t\t}\n   601→\n   602→\t\t// If we reach this path, this means that a namespace has been both defined in Git, as well in the\n   603→\t\t// application's managedNamespaceMetadata. We want to ensure that this manifest is the one being used instead\n   604→\t\t// of what is present in managedNamespaceMetadata.\n   605→\t\tif isManagedNamespace(targetObj, app) {\n   606→\t\t\ttargetNsExists = true\n   607→\t\t}\n   608→\t}\n   609→\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"dedup_ms\")\n   610→\n   611→\tliveObjByKey, err := m.liveStateCache.GetManagedLiveObjs(app, targetObjs)\n   612→\tif err != nil {\n   613→\t\tliveObjByKey = make(map[kubeutil.ResourceKey]*unstructured.Unstructured)\n   614→\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to load live state: %s\", err.Error())\n   615→\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   616→\t\tfailedToLoadObjs = true\n   617→\t}\n   618→\n   619→\tlogCtx.Debugf(\"Retrieved live manifests\")\n   620→\n   621→\t// filter out all resources which are not permitted in the application project\n   622→\tfor k, v := range liveObjByKey {\n   623→\t\tpermitted, err := project.IsLiveResourcePermitted(v, app.Spec.Destination.Server, app.Spec.Destination.Name, func(project string) ([]*v1alpha1.Cluster, error) {\n   624→\t\t\tclusters, err := m.db.GetProjectClusters(context.TODO(), project)\n   625→\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   626→\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get clusters for project %q: %w\", project, %s\", k.String(), app.Spec.Project, err.Error())\n   632→\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   633→\t\t\tfailedToLoadObjs = true\n   634→\t\t\tcontinue\n   635→\t\t}\n   636→\n   637→\t\tif !permitted {\n   638→\t\t\tdelete(liveObjByKey, k)\n   639→\t\t}\n   640→\t}\n   641→\n   642→\ttrackingMethod := argo.GetTrackingMethod(m.settingsMgr)\n   643→\n   644→\tfor _, liveObj := range liveObjByKey {\n   645→\t\tif liveObj != nil {\n   646→\t\t\tappInstanceName := m.resourceTracking.GetAppName(liveObj, appLabelKey, trackingMethod, installationID)\n   647→\t\t\tif appInstanceName != \"\" && appInstanceName != app.InstanceName(m.namespace) {\n   648→\t\t\t\tfqInstanceName := strings.ReplaceAll(appInstanceName, \"_\", \"/\")\n   649→\t\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{\n   650→\t\t\t\t\tType:               v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionSharedResourceWarning,\n   651→\t\t\t\t\tMessage:            fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s is part of applications %s and %s\", liveObj.GetKind(), liveObj.GetName(), app.QualifiedName(), fqInstanceName),\n   652→\t\t\t\t\tLastTransitionTime: &now,\n   653→\t\t\t\t})\n   654→\t\t\t}\n   655→\n   656→\t\t\t// For the case when a namespace is managed with `managedNamespaceMetadata` AND it has resource tracking\n   657→\t\t\t// enabled (e.g. someone manually adds resource tracking labels or annotations), we need to do some\n   658→\t\t\t// bookkeeping in order to prevent the managed namespace from being pruned.\n   659→\t\t\t//\n   660→\t\t\t// Live namespaces which are managed namespaces (i.e. application namespaces which are managed with\n   661→\t\t\t// CreateNamespace=true and has non-nil managedNamespaceMetadata) will (usually) not have a corresponding\n   662→\t\t\t// entry in source control. In order for the namespace not to risk being pruned, we'll need to generate a\n   663→\t\t\t// namespace which we can compare the live namespace with. For that, we'll do the same as is done in\n   664→\t\t\t// gitops-engine, the difference here being that we create a managed namespace which is only used for comparison.\n   665→\t\t\t//\n   666→\t\t\t// targetNsExists == true implies that it already exists as a target, so no need to add the namespace to the\n   667→\t\t\t// targetObjs array.\n   668→\t\t\tif isManagedNamespace(liveObj, app) && !targetNsExists {\n   669→\t\t\t\tnsSpec := &v1.Namespace{TypeMeta: metav1.TypeMeta{APIVersion: \"v1\", Kind: kubeutil.NamespaceKind}, ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: liveObj.GetName()}}\n   670→\t\t\t\tmanagedNs, err := kubeutil.ToUnstructured(nsSpec)\n   671→\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   672→\t\t\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: err.Error(), LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   673→\t\t\t\t\tfailedToLoadObjs = true\n   674→\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   675→\t\t\t\t}\n   676→\n   677→\t\t\t\t// No need to care about the return value here, we just want the modified managedNs\n   678→\t\t\t\t_, err = syncNamespace(app.Spec.SyncPolicy)(managedNs, liveObj)\n   679→\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   680→\t\t\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: err.Error(), LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   681→\t\t\t\t\tfailedToLoadObjs = true\n   682→\t\t\t\t} else {\n   683→\t\t\t\t\ttargetObjs = append(targetObjs, managedNs)\n   684→\t\t\t\t}\n   685→\t\t\t}\n   686→\t\t}\n   687→\t}\n   688→\thasPostDeleteHooks := false\n   689→\tfor _, obj := range targetObjs {\n   690→\t\tif isPostDeleteHook(obj) {\n   691→\t\t\thasPostDeleteHooks = true\n   692→\t\t}\n   693→\t}\n   694→\n   695→\treconciliation := sync.Reconcile(targetObjs, liveObjByKey, app.Spec.Destination.Namespace, infoProvider)\n   696→\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"live_ms\")\n   697→\n   698→\tcompareOptions, err := m.settingsMgr.GetResourceCompareOptions()\n   699→\tif err != nil {\n   700→\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Could not get compare options from ConfigMap (assuming defaults): %v\", err)\n   701→\t\tcompareOptions = settings.GetDefaultDiffOptions()\n   702→\t}\n   703→\tmanifestRevisions := make([]string, 0)\n   704→\n   705→\tfor _, manifestInfo := range manifestInfos {\n   706→\t\tmanifestRevisions = append(manifestRevisions, manifestInfo.Revision)\n   707→\t}\n   708→\n   709→\tserverSideDiff := m.serverSideDiff ||\n   710→\t\tresourceutil.HasAnnotationOption(app, common.AnnotationCompareOptions, \"ServerSideDiff=true\")\n   711→\n   712→\t// This allows turning SSD off for a given app if it is enabled at the\n   713→\t// controller level\n   714→\tif resourceutil.HasAnnotationOption(app, common.AnnotationCompareOptions, \"ServerSideDiff=false\") {\n   715→\t\tserverSideDiff = false\n   716→\t}\n   717→\n   718→\tuseDiffCache := useDiffCache(noCache, manifestInfos, sources, app, manifestRevisions, m.statusRefreshTimeout, serverSideDiff, logCtx)\n   719→\n   720→\tdiffConfigBuilder := argodiff.NewDiffConfigBuilder().\n   721→\t\tWithDiffSettings(app.Spec.IgnoreDifferences, resourceOverrides, compareOptions.IgnoreAggregatedRoles, m.ignoreNormalizerOpts).\n   722→\t\tWithTracking(appLabelKey, string(trackingMethod))\n   723→\n   724→\tif useDiffCache {\n   725→\t\tdiffConfigBuilder.WithCache(m.cache, app.InstanceName(m.namespace))\n   726→\t} else {\n   727→\t\tdiffConfigBuilder.WithNoCache()\n   728→\t}\n   729→\n   730→\tif resourceutil.HasAnnotationOption(app, common.AnnotationCompareOptions, \"IncludeMutationWebhook=true\") {\n   731→\t\tdiffConfigBuilder.WithIgnoreMutationWebhook(false)\n   732→\t}\n   733→\n   734→\tgvkParser, err := m.getGVKParser(app.Spec.Destination.Server)\n   735→\tif err != nil {\n   736→\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionUnknownError, Message: err.Error(), LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   737→\t}\n   738→\tdiffConfigBuilder.WithGVKParser(gvkParser)\n   739→\tdiffConfigBuilder.WithManager(common.ArgoCDSSAManager)\n   740→\n   741→\tdiffConfigBuilder.WithServerSideDiff(serverSideDiff)\n   742→\n   743→\tif serverSideDiff {\n   744→\t\tapplier, cleanup, err := m.getServerSideDiffDryRunApplier(app.Spec.Destination.Server)\n   745→\t\tif err != nil {\n   746→\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"CompareAppState error getting server side diff dry run applier: %s\", err)\n   747→\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionUnknownError, Message: err.Error(), LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   748→\t\t}\n   749→\t\tdefer cleanup()\n   750→\t\tdiffConfigBuilder.WithServerSideDryRunner(diff.NewK8sServerSideDryRunner(applier))\n   751→\t}\n   752→\n   753→\t// enable structured merge diff if application syncs with server-side apply\n   754→\tif app.Spec.SyncPolicy != nil && app.Spec.SyncPolicy.SyncOptions.HasOption(\"ServerSideApply=true\") {\n   755→\t\tdiffConfigBuilder.WithStructuredMergeDiff(true)\n   756→\t}\n   757→\n   758→\t// it is necessary to ignore the error at this point to avoid creating duplicated\n   759→\t// application conditions as argo.StateDiffs will validate this diffConfig again.\n   760→\tdiffConfig, _ := diffConfigBuilder.Build()\n   761→\n   762→\tdiffResults, err := argodiff.StateDiffs(reconciliation.Live, reconciliation.Target, diffConfig)\n   763→\tif err != nil {\n   764→\t\tdiffResults = &diff.DiffResultList{}\n   765→\t\tfailedToLoadObjs = true\n   766→\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to compare desired state to live state: %s\", err.Error())\n   767→\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   768→\t}\n   769→\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"diff_ms\")\n   770→\n   771→\tsyncCode := v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeSynced\n   772→\tmanagedResources := make([]managedResource, len(reconciliation.Target))\n   773→\tresourceSummaries := make([]v1alpha1.ResourceStatus, len(reconciliation.Target))\n   774→\tfor i, targetObj := range reconciliation.Target {\n   775→\t\tliveObj := reconciliation.Live[i]\n   776→\t\tobj := liveObj\n   777→\t\tif obj == nil {\n   778→\t\t\tobj = targetObj\n   779→\t\t}\n   780→\t\tif obj == nil {\n   781→\t\t\tcontinue\n   782→\t\t}\n   783→\t\tgvk := obj.GroupVersionKind()\n   784→\n   785→\t\tisSelfReferencedObj := m.isSelfReferencedObj(liveObj, targetObj, app.GetName(), appLabelKey, trackingMethod, installationID)\n   786→\n   787→\t\tresState := v1alpha1.ResourceStatus{\n   788→\t\t\tNamespace:       obj.GetNamespace(),\n   789→\t\t\tName:            obj.GetName(),\n   790→\t\t\tKind:            gvk.Kind,\n   791→\t\t\tVersion:         gvk.Version,\n   792→\t\t\tGroup:           gvk.Group,\n   793→\t\t\tHook:            isHook(obj),\n   794→\t\t\tRequiresPruning: targetObj == nil && liveObj != nil && isSelfReferencedObj,\n   795→\t\t\tRequiresDeletionConfirmation: targetObj != nil && resourceutil.HasAnnotationOption(targetObj, synccommon.AnnotationSyncOptions, synccommon.SyncOptionDeleteRequireConfirm) ||\n   796→\t\t\t\tliveObj != nil && resourceutil.HasAnnotationOption(liveObj, synccommon.AnnotationSyncOptions, synccommon.SyncOptionDeleteRequireConfirm),\n   797→\t\t}\n   798→\t\tif targetObj != nil {\n   799→\t\t\tresState.SyncWave = int64(syncwaves.Wave(targetObj))\n   800→\t\t}\n   801→\n   802→\t\tvar diffResult diff.DiffResult\n   803→\t\tif i < len(diffResults.Diffs) {\n   804→\t\t\tdiffResult = diffResults.Diffs[i]\n   805→\t\t} else {\n   806→\t\t\tdiffResult = diff.DiffResult{Modified: false, NormalizedLive: []byte(\"{}\"), PredictedLive: []byte(\"{}\")}\n   807→\t\t}\n   808→\n   809→\t\t// For the case when a namespace is managed with `managedNamespaceMetadata` AND it has resource tracking\n   810→\t\t// enabled (e.g. someone manually adds resource tracking labels or annotations), we need to do some\n   811→\t\t// bookkeeping in order to ensure that it's not considered `OutOfSync` (since it does not exist in source\n   812→\t\t// control).\n   813→\t\t//\n   814→\t\t// This is in addition to the bookkeeping we do (see `isManagedNamespace` and its references) to prevent said\n   815→\t\t// namespace from being pruned.\n   816→\t\tisManagedNs := isManagedNamespace(targetObj, app) && liveObj == nil\n   817→\n   818→\t\tif resState.Hook || ignore.Ignore(obj) || (targetObj != nil && hookutil.Skip(targetObj)) || !isSelfReferencedObj {\n   819→\t\t\t// For resource hooks, skipped resources or objects that may have\n   820→\t\t\t// been created by another controller with annotations copied from\n   821→\t\t\t// the source object, don't store sync status, and do not affect\n   822→\t\t\t// overall sync status\n   823→\t\t} else if !isManagedNs && (diffResult.Modified || targetObj == nil || liveObj == nil) {\n   824→\t\t\t// Set resource state to OutOfSync since one of the following is true:\n   825→\t\t\t// * target and live resource are different\n   826→\t\t\t// * target resource not defined and live resource is extra\n   827→\t\t\t// * target resource present but live resource is missing\n   828→\t\t\tresState.Status = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeOutOfSync\n   829→\t\t\t// we ignore the status if the obj needs pruning AND we have the annotation\n   830→\t\t\tneedsPruning := targetObj == nil && liveObj != nil\n   831→\t\t\tif !(needsPruning && resourceutil.HasAnnotationOption(obj, common.AnnotationCompareOptions, \"IgnoreExtraneous\")) {\n   832→\t\t\t\tsyncCode = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeOutOfSync\n   833→\t\t\t}\n   834→\t\t} else {\n   835→\t\t\tresState.Status = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeSynced\n   836→\t\t}\n   837→\t\t// set unknown status to all resource that are not permitted in the app project\n   838→\t\tisNamespaced, err := m.liveStateCache.IsNamespaced(app.Spec.Destination.Server, gvk.GroupKind())\n   839→\t\tif !project.IsGroupKindPermitted(gvk.GroupKind(), isNamespaced && err == nil) {\n   840→\t\t\tresState.Status = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeUnknown\n   841→\t\t}\n   842→\n   843→\t\tif isNamespaced && obj.GetNamespace() == \"\" {\n   844→\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionInvalidSpecError, Message: fmt.Sprintf(\"Namespace for %s %s is missing.\", obj.GetName(), gvk.String()), LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   845→\t\t}\n   846→\n   847→\t\t// we can't say anything about the status if we were unable to get the target objects\n   848→\t\tif failedToLoadObjs {\n   849→\t\t\tresState.Status = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeUnknown\n   850→\t\t}\n   851→\n   852→\t\tresourceVersion := \"\"\n   853→\t\tif liveObj != nil {\n   854→\t\t\tresourceVersion = liveObj.GetResourceVersion()\n   855→\t\t}\n   856→\t\tmanagedResources[i] = managedResource{\n   857→\t\t\tName:            resState.Name,\n   858→\t\t\tNamespace:       resState.Namespace,\n   859→\t\t\tGroup:           resState.Group,\n   860→\t\t\tKind:            resState.Kind,\n   861→\t\t\tVersion:         resState.Version,\n   862→\t\t\tLive:            liveObj,\n   863→\t\t\tTarget:          targetObj,\n   864→\t\t\tDiff:            diffResult,\n   865→\t\t\tHook:            resState.Hook,\n   866→\t\t\tResourceVersion: resourceVersion,\n   867→\t\t}\n   868→\t\tresourceSummaries[i] = resState\n   869→\t}\n   870→\n   871→\tif failedToLoadObjs {\n   872→\t\tsyncCode = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeUnknown\n   873→\t} else if app.HasChangedManagedNamespaceMetadata() {\n   874→\t\tsyncCode = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeOutOfSync\n   875→\t}\n   876→\tvar revision string\n   877→\n   878→\tif !hasMultipleSources && len(manifestRevisions) > 0 {\n   879→\t\trevision = manifestRevisions[0]\n   880→\t}\n   881→\tvar syncStatus v1alpha1.SyncStatus\n   882→\tif hasMultipleSources {\n   883→\t\tsyncStatus = v1alpha1.SyncStatus{\n   884→\t\t\tComparedTo: v1alpha1.ComparedTo{\n   885→\t\t\t\tDestination:       app.Spec.Destination,\n   886→\t\t\t\tSources:           sources,\n   887→\t\t\t\tIgnoreDifferences: app.Spec.IgnoreDifferences,\n   888→\t\t\t},\n   889→\t\t\tStatus:    syncCode,\n   890→\t\t\tRevisions: manifestRevisions,\n   891→\t\t}\n   892→\t} else {\n   893→\t\tsyncStatus = v1alpha1.SyncStatus{\n   894→\t\t\tComparedTo: v1alpha1.ComparedTo{\n   895→\t\t\t\tDestination:       app.Spec.Destination,\n   896→\t\t\t\tSource:            app.Spec.GetSource(),\n   897→\t\t\t\tIgnoreDifferences: app.Spec.IgnoreDifferences,\n   898→\t\t\t},\n   899→\t\t\tStatus:   syncCode,\n   900→\t\t\tRevision: revision,\n   901→\t\t}\n   902→\t}\n   903→\n   904→\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"sync_ms\")\n   905→\n   906→\thealthStatus, err := setApplicationHealth(managedResources, resourceSummaries, resourceOverrides, app, m.persistResourceHealth)\n   907→\tif err != nil {\n   908→\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: fmt.Sprintf(\"error setting app health: %s\", err.Error()), LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   909→\t}\n   910→\n   911→\t// Git has already performed the signature verification via its GPG interface, and the result is available\n   912→\t// in the manifest info received from the repository server. We now need to form our opinion about the result\n   913→\t// and stop processing if we do not agree about the outcome.\n   914→\tfor _, manifestInfo := range manifestInfos {\n   915→\t\tif gpg.IsGPGEnabled() && verifySignature && manifestInfo != nil {\n   916→\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, verifyGnuPGSignature(manifestInfo.Revision, project, manifestInfo)...)\n   917→\t\t}\n   918→\t}\n   919→\n   920→\tcompRes := comparisonResult{\n   921→\t\tsyncStatus:           &syncStatus,\n   922→\t\thealthStatus:         healthStatus,\n   923→\t\tresources:            resourceSummaries,\n   924→\t\tmanagedResources:     managedResources,\n   925→\t\treconciliationResult: reconciliation,\n   926→\t\tdiffConfig:           diffConfig,\n   927→\t\tdiffResultList:       diffResults,\n   928→\t\thasPostDeleteHooks:   hasPostDeleteHooks,\n   929→\t\trevisionUpdated:      revisionUpdated,\n   930→\t}\n   931→\n   932→\tif hasMultipleSources {\n   933→\t\tfor _, manifestInfo := range manifestInfos {\n   934→\t\t\tcompRes.appSourceTypes = append(compRes.appSourceTypes, v1alpha1.ApplicationSourceType(manifestInfo.SourceType))\n   935→\t\t}\n   936→\t} else {\n   937→\t\tfor _, manifestInfo := range manifestInfos {\n   938→\t\t\tcompRes.appSourceType = v1alpha1.ApplicationSourceType(manifestInfo.SourceType)\n   939→\t\t\tbreak\n   940→\t\t}\n   941→\t}\n   942→\n   943→\tapp.Status.SetConditions(conditions, map[v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionType]bool{\n   944→\t\tv1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError:         true,\n   945→\t\tv1alpha1.ApplicationConditionSharedResourceWarning:   true,\n   946→\t\tv1alpha1.ApplicationConditionRepeatedResourceWarning: true,\n   947→\t\tv1alpha1.ApplicationConditionExcludedResourceWarning: true,\n   948→\t})\n   949→\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"health_ms\")\n   950→\tcompRes.timings = ts.Timings()\n   951→\treturn &compRes, nil\n   952→}\n   953→\n   954→// useDiffCache will determine if the diff should be calculated based\n   955→// on the existing live state cache or not.\n   956→func useDiffCache(noCache bool, manifestInfos []*apiclient.ManifestResponse, sources []v1alpha1.ApplicationSource, app *v1alpha1.Application, manifestRevisions []string, statusRefreshTimeout time.Duration, serverSideDiff bool, log *log.Entry) bool {\n   957→\tif noCache {\n   958→\t\tlog.WithField(\"useDiffCache\", \"false\").Debug(\"noCache is true\")\n   959→\t\treturn false\n   960→\t}\n   961→\trefreshType, refreshRequested := app.IsRefreshRequested()\n   962→\tif refreshRequested {\n   963→\t\tlog.WithField(\"useDiffCache\", \"false\").Debugf(\"refresh type %s requested\", string(refreshType))\n   964→\t\treturn false\n   965→\t}\n   966→\t// serverSideDiff should still use cache even if status is expired.\n   967→\t// This is an attempt to avoid hitting k8s API server too frequently during\n   968→\t// app refresh with serverSideDiff is enabled. If there are negative side\n   969→\t// effects identified with this approach, the serverSideDiff should be removed\n   970→\t// from this condition.\n   971→\tif app.Status.Expired(statusRefreshTimeout) && !serverSideDiff {\n   972→\t\tlog.WithField(\"useDiffCache\", \"false\").Debug(\"app.status.expired\")\n   973→\t\treturn false\n   974→\t}\n   975→\n   976→\tif len(manifestInfos) != len(sources) {\n   977→\t\tlog.WithField(\"useDiffCache\", \"false\").Debug(\"manifestInfos len != sources len\")\n   978→\t\treturn false\n   979→\t}\n   980→\n   981→\trevisionChanged := !reflect.DeepEqual(app.Status.GetRevisions(), manifestRevisions)\n   982→\tif revisionChanged {\n   983→\t\tlog.WithField(\"useDiffCache\", \"false\").Debug(\"revisionChanged\")\n   984→\t\treturn false\n   985→\t}\n   986→\n   987→\tif !specEqualsCompareTo(app.Spec, app.Status.Sync.ComparedTo) {\n   988→\t\tlog.WithField(\"useDiffCache\", \"false\").Debug(\"specChanged\")\n   989→\t\treturn false\n   990→\t}\n   991→\n   992→\tlog.WithField(\"useDiffCache\", \"true\").Debug(\"using diff cache\")\n   993→\treturn true\n   994→}\n   995→\n   996→// specEqualsCompareTo compares the application spec to the comparedTo status. It normalizes the destination to match\n   997→// the comparedTo destination before comparing. It does not mutate the original spec or comparedTo.\n   998→func specEqualsCompareTo(spec v1alpha1.ApplicationSpec, comparedTo v1alpha1.ComparedTo) bool {\n   999→\t// Make a copy to be sure we don't mutate the original.\n  1000→\tspecCopy := spec.DeepCopy()\n  1001→\tcurrentSpec := specCopy.BuildComparedToStatus()\n  1002→\n  1003→\t// The spec might have been augmented to include both server and name, so change it to match the comparedTo before\n  1004→\t// comparing.\n  1005→\tif comparedTo.Destination.Server == \"\" {\n  1006→\t\tcurrentSpec.Destination.Server = \"\"\n  1007→\t}\n  1008→\tif comparedTo.Destination.Name == \"\" {\n  1009→\t\tcurrentSpec.Destination.Name = \"\"\n  1010→\t}\n  1011→\n  1012→\t// Set IsServerInferred to false on both, because that field is not important for comparison.\n  1013→\tcomparedTo.Destination.SetIsServerInferred(false)\n  1014→\tcurrentSpec.Destination.SetIsServerInferred(false)\n  1015→\n  1016→\treturn reflect.DeepEqual(comparedTo, currentSpec)\n  1017→}\n  1018→\n  1019→func (m *appStateManager) persistRevisionHistory(\n  1020→\tapp *v1alpha1.Application,\n  1021→\trevision string,\n  1022→\tsource v1alpha1.ApplicationSource,\n  1023→\trevisions []string,\n  1024→\tsources []v1alpha1.ApplicationSource,\n  1025→\thasMultipleSources bool,\n  1026→\tstartedAt metav1.Time,\n  1027→\tinitiatedBy v1alpha1.OperationInitiator,\n  1028→) error {\n  1029→\tvar nextID int64\n  1030→\tif len(app.Status.History) > 0 {\n  1031→\t\tnextID = app.Status.History.LastRevisionHistory().ID + 1\n  1032→\t}\n  1033→\n  1034→\tif hasMultipleSources {\n  1035→\t\tapp.Status.History = append(app.Status.History, v1alpha1.RevisionHistory{\n  1036→\t\t\tDeployedAt:      metav1.NewTime(time.Now().UTC()),\n  1037→\t\t\tDeployStartedAt: &startedAt,\n  1038→\t\t\tID:              nextID,\n  1039→\t\t\tSources:         sources,\n  1040→\t\t\tRevisions:       revisions,\n  1041→\t\t\tInitiatedBy:     initiatedBy,\n  1042→\t\t})\n  1043→\t} else {\n  1044→\t\tapp.Status.History = append(app.Status.History, v1alpha1.RevisionHistory{\n  1045→\t\t\tRevision:        revision,\n  1046→\t\t\tDeployedAt:      metav1.NewTime(time.Now().UTC()),\n  1047→\t\t\tDeployStartedAt: &startedAt,\n  1048→\t\t\tID:              nextID,\n  1049→\t\t\tSource:          source,\n  1050→\t\t\tInitiatedBy:     initiatedBy,\n  1051→\t\t})\n  1052→\t}\n  1053→\n  1054→\tapp.Status.History = app.Status.History.Trunc(app.Spec.GetRevisionHistoryLimit())\n  1055→\n  1056→\tpatch, err := json.Marshal(map[string]map[string][]v1alpha1.RevisionHistory{\n  1057→\t\t\"status\": {\n  1058→\t\t\t\"history\": app.Status.History,\n  1059→\t\t},\n  1060→\t})\n  1061→\tif err != nil {\n  1062→\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error marshaling revision history patch: %w\", err)\n  1063→\t}\n  1064→\t_, err = m.appclientset.ArgoprojV1alpha1().Applications(app.Namespace).Patch(context.Background(), app.Name, types.MergePatchType, patch, metav1.PatchOptions{})\n  1065→\treturn err\n  1066→}\n  1067→\n  1068→// NewAppStateManager creates new instance of AppStateManager\n  1069→func NewAppStateManager(\n  1070→\tdb db.ArgoDB,\n  1071→\tappclientset appclientset.Interface,\n  1072→\trepoClientset apiclient.Clientset,\n  1073→\tnamespace string,\n  1074→\tkubectl kubeutil.Kubectl,\n  1075→\tonKubectlRun kubeutil.OnKubectlRunFunc,\n  1076→\tsettingsMgr *settings.SettingsManager,\n  1077→\tliveStateCache statecache.LiveStateCache,\n  1078→\tprojInformer cache.SharedIndexInformer,\n  1079→\tmetricsServer *metrics.MetricsServer,\n  1080→\tcache *appstatecache.Cache,\n  1081→\tstatusRefreshTimeout time.Duration,\n  1082→\tresourceTracking argo.ResourceTracking,\n  1083→\tpersistResourceHealth bool,\n  1084→\trepoErrorGracePeriod time.Duration,\n  1085→\tserverSideDiff bool,\n  1086→\tignoreNormalizerOpts normalizers.IgnoreNormalizerOpts,\n  1087→) AppStateManager {\n  1088→\treturn &appStateManager{\n  1089→\t\tliveStateCache:        liveStateCache,\n  1090→\t\tcache:                 cache,\n  1091→\t\tdb:                    db,\n  1092→\t\tappclientset:          appclientset,\n  1093→\t\tkubectl:               kubectl,\n  1094→\t\tonKubectlRun:          onKubectlRun,\n  1095→\t\trepoClientset:         repoClientset,\n  1096→\t\tnamespace:             namespace,\n  1097→\t\tsettingsMgr:           settingsMgr,\n  1098→\t\tprojInformer:          projInformer,\n  1099→\t\tmetricsServer:         metricsServer,\n  1100→\t\tstatusRefreshTimeout:  statusRefreshTimeout,\n  1101→\t\tresourceTracking:      resourceTracking,\n  1102→\t\tpersistResourceHealth: persistResourceHealth,\n  1103→\t\trepoErrorGracePeriod:  repoErrorGracePeriod,\n  1104→\t\tserverSideDiff:        serverSideDiff,\n  1105→\t\tignoreNormalizerOpts:  ignoreNormalizerOpts,\n  1106→\t}\n  1107→}\n  1108→\n  1109→// isSelfReferencedObj returns whether the given obj is managed by the application\n  1110→// according to the values of the tracking id (aka app instance value) annotation.\n  1111→// It returns true when all of the properties of the tracking id (app name, namespace,\n  1112→// group and kind) match the properties of the live object, or if the tracking method\n  1113→// used does not provide the required properties for matching.\n  1114→// Reference: https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/8683\n  1115→func (m *appStateManager) isSelfReferencedObj(live, config *unstructured.Unstructured, appName, appLabelKey string, trackingMethod v1alpha1.TrackingMethod, installationID string) bool {\n  1116→\tif live == nil {\n  1117→\t\treturn true\n  1118→\t}\n  1119→\n  1120→\t// If tracking method doesn't contain required metadata for this check,\n  1121→\t// we are not able to determine and just assume the object to be managed.\n  1122→\tif trackingMethod == argo.TrackingMethodLabel {\n  1123→\t\treturn true\n  1124→\t}\n  1125→\n  1126→\t// config != nil is the best-case scenario for constructing an accurate\n  1127→\t// Tracking ID. `config` is the \"desired state\" (from git/helm/etc.).\n  1128→\t// Using the desired state is important when there is an ApiGroup upgrade.\n  1129→\t// When upgrading, the comparison must be made with the new tracking ID.\n  1130→\t// Example:\n  1131→\t//     live resource annotation will be:\n  1132→\t//        ingress-app:extensions/Ingress:default/some-ingress\n  1133→\t//     when it should be:\n  1134→\t//        ingress-app:networking.k8s.io/Ingress:default/some-ingress\n  1135→\t// More details in: https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/pull/11012\n  1136→\tvar aiv argo.AppInstanceValue\n  1137→\tif config != nil {\n  1138→\t\taiv = argo.UnstructuredToAppInstanceValue(config, appName, \"\")\n  1139→\t\treturn isSelfReferencedObj(live, aiv)\n  1140→\t}\n  1141→\n  1142→\t// If config is nil then compare the live resource with the value\n  1143→\t// of the annotation. In this case, in order to validate if obj is\n  1144→\t// managed by this application, the values from the annotation have\n  1145→\t// to match the properties from the live object. Cluster scoped objects\n  1146→\t// carry the app's destination namespace in the tracking annotation,\n  1147→\t// but are unique in GVK + name combination.\n  1148→\tappInstance := m.resourceTracking.GetAppInstance(live, appLabelKey, trackingMethod, installationID)\n  1149→\tif appInstance != nil {\n  1150→\t\treturn isSelfReferencedObj(live, *appInstance)\n  1151→\t}\n  1152→\treturn true\n  1153→}\n  1154→\n  1155→// isSelfReferencedObj returns true if the given Tracking ID (`aiv`) matches\n  1156→// the given object. It returns false when the ID doesn't match. This sometimes\n  1157→// happens when a tracking label or annotation gets accidentally copied to a\n  1158→// different resource.\n  1159→func isSelfReferencedObj(obj *unstructured.Unstructured, aiv argo.AppInstanceValue) bool {\n  1160→\treturn (obj.GetNamespace() == aiv.Namespace || obj.GetNamespace() == \"\") &&\n  1161→\t\tobj.GetName() == aiv.Name &&\n  1162→\t\tobj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind().Group == aiv.Group &&\n  1163→\t\tobj.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind().Kind == aiv.Kind\n  1164→}\n  1165→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 269→}\n   270→\n   271→func asResourceNode(r *clustercache.Resource) appv1.ResourceNode {\n   272→\tgv, err := schema.ParseGroupVersion(r.Ref.APIVersion)\n   273→\tif err != nil {\n   274→\t\tgv = schema.GroupVersion{}\n   275→\t}\n   276→\tparentRefs := make([]appv1.ResourceRef, len(r.OwnerRefs))\n   277→\tfor i, ownerRef := range r.OwnerRefs {\n   278→\t\townerGvk :=
