appv1.ResourceRef{Name: ownerRef.Name, Kind: ownerKey.Kind, Namespace: r.Ref.Namespace, Group: ownerKey.Group, UID: string(ownerRef.UID)}\n   281→\t}\n   282→\tvar resHealth *appv1.HealthStatus\n   283→\tresourceInfo := resInfo(r)\n   284→\tif resourceInfo.Health != nil {\n   285→\t\tresHealth = &appv1.HealthStatus{Status: resourceInfo.Health.Status, Message: resourceInfo.Health.Message}\n   286→\t}\n   287→\treturn appv1.ResourceNode{\n   288→\t\tResourceRef: appv1.ResourceRef{\n   289→\t\t\tUID:       string(r.Ref.UID),\n   290→\t\t\tName:      r.Ref.Name,\n   291→\t\t\tGroup:     gv.Group,\n   292→\t\t\tVersion:   gv.Version,\n   293→\t\t\tKind:      r.Ref.Kind,\n   294→\t\t\tNamespace: r.Ref.Namespace,\n   295→\t\t},\n   296→\t\tParentRefs:      parentRefs,\n   297→\t\tInfo:            resourceInfo.Info,\n   298→\t\tResourceVersion: r.ResourceVersion,\n   299→\t\tNetworkingInfo:  resourceInfo.NetworkingInfo,\n   300→\t\tImages:          resourceInfo.Images,\n   301→\t\tHealth:          resHealth,\n   302→\t\tCreatedAt:       r.CreationTimestamp,\n   303→\t}\n   304→}\n   305→\n   306→func resInfo(r *clustercache.Resource) *ResourceInfo {\n   307→\tinfo, ok := r.Info.(*ResourceInfo)\n   308→\tif !ok || info == nil {\n   309→\t\tinfo = &ResourceInfo{}\n   310→\t}\n   311→\treturn info\n   312→}\n   313→\n   314→func isRootAppNode(r *clustercache.Resource) bool {\n   315→\treturn resInfo(r).AppName != \"\" && len(r.OwnerRefs) == 0\n   316→}\n   317→\n   318→func getApp(r *clustercache.Resource, ns map[kube.ResourceKey]*clustercache.Resource) string {\n   319→\tname, _ := getAppRecursive(r, ns, map[kube.ResourceKey]bool{})\n   320→\treturn name\n   321→}\n   322→\n   323→func ownerRefGV(ownerRef metav1.OwnerReference) schema.GroupVersion {\n   324→\tgv, err := schema.ParseGroupVersion(ownerRef.APIVersion)\n   325→\tif err != nil {\n   326→\t\tgv = schema.GroupVersion{}\n   327→\t}\n   328→\treturn gv\n   329→}\n   330→\n   331→func getAppRecursive(r *clustercache.Resource, ns map[kube.ResourceKey]*clustercache.Resource, visited map[kube.ResourceKey]bool) (string, bool) {\n   332→\tif !visited[r.ResourceKey()] {\n   333→\t\tvisited[r.ResourceKey()] = true\n   334→\t} else {\n   335→\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Circular dependency detected: %v.\", visited)\n   336→\t\treturn resInfo(r).AppName, false\n   337→\t}\n   338→\n   339→\tif resInfo(r).AppName != \"\" {\n   340→\t\treturn resInfo(r).AppName, true\n   341→\t}\n   342→\tfor _, ownerRef := range r.OwnerRefs {\n   343→\t\tgv := ownerRefGV(ownerRef)\n   344→\t\tif parent, ok := ns[kube.NewResourceKey(gv.Group, ownerRef.Kind, r.Ref.Namespace, ownerRef.Name)]; ok {\n   345→\t\t\tvisited_branch := make(map[kube.ResourceKey]bool, len(visited))\n   346→\t\t\tfor k, v := range visited {\n   347→\t\t\t\tvisited_branch[k] = v\n   348→\t\t\t}\n   349→\t\t\tapp, ok := getAppRecursive(parent, ns, visited_branch)\n   350→\t\t\tif app != \"\" || !ok {\n   351→\t\t\t\treturn app, ok\n   352→\t\t\t}\n   353→\t\t}\n   354→\t}\n   355→\treturn \"\", true\n   356→}\n   357→\n   358→var ignoredRefreshResources = map[string]bool{\n   359→\t\"/\" + kube.EndpointsKind: true,\n   360→}\n   361→\n   362→// skipAppRequeuing checks if the object is an API type which we want to skip requeuing against.\n   363→// We ignore API types which have a high churn rate, and/or whose updates are irrelevant to the app\n   364→func skipAppRequeuing(key kube.ResourceKey) bool {\n   365→\treturn ignoredRefreshResources[key.Group+\"/\"+key.Kind]\n   366→}\n   367→\n   368→func skipResourceUpdate(oldInfo, newInfo *ResourceInfo) bool {\n   369→\tif oldInfo == nil || newInfo == nil {\n   370→\t\treturn false\n   371→\t}\n   372→\tisSameHealthStatus := (oldInfo.Health == nil && newInfo.Health == nil) || oldInfo.Health != nil && newInfo.Health != nil && oldInfo.Health.Status == newInfo.Health.Status\n   373→\tisSameManifest := oldInfo.manifestHash != \"\" && newInfo.manifestHash != \"\" && oldInfo.manifestHash == newInfo.manifestHash\n   374→\treturn isSameHealthStatus && isSameManifest\n   375→}\n   376→\n   377→// shouldHashManifest validates if the API resource needs to be hashed.\n   378→// If there's an app name from resource tracking, or if this is itself an app, we should generate a hash.\n   379→// Otherwise, the hashing should be skipped to save CPU time.\n   380→func shouldHashManifest(appName string, gvk schema.GroupVersionKind, un *unstructured.Unstructured) bool {\n   381→\t// Only hash if the resource belongs to an app OR argocd.argoproj.io/ignore-resource-updates is present and set to true\n   382→\t// Best      - Only hash for resources that are part of an app or their dependencies\n   383→\t// (current) - Only hash for resources that are part of an app + all apps that might be from an ApplicationSet\n   384→\t// Orphan    - If orphan is enabled, hash should be made on all resource of that namespace and a config to disable it\n   385→\t// Worst     - Hash all resources watched by Argo\n   386→\tisTrackedResource := appName != \"\" || (gvk.Group == application.Group && gvk.Kind == application.ApplicationKind)\n   387→\n   388→\t// If the resource is not a tracked resource, we will look up argocd.argoproj.io/ignore-resource-updates and decide\n   389→\t// whether we generate hash or not.\n   390→\t// If argocd.argoproj.io/ignore-resource-updates is presented and is true, return true\n   391→\t// Else return false\n   392→\tif !isTrackedResource {\n   393→\t\tif val, ok := un.GetAnnotations()[AnnotationIgnoreResourceUpdates]; ok {\n   394→\t\t\tapplyResourcesUpdate, err := strconv.ParseBool(val)\n   395→\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   396→\t\t\t\tapplyResourcesUpdate = false\n   397→\t\t\t}\n   398→\t\t\treturn applyResourcesUpdate\n   399→\t\t}\n   400→\t\treturn false\n   401→\t}\n   402→\n   403→\treturn isTrackedResource\n   404→}\n   405→\n   406→// isRetryableError is a helper method to see whether an error\n   407→// returned from the dynamic client is potentially retryable.\n   408→func isRetryableError(err error) bool {\n   409→\tif err == nil {\n   410→\t\treturn false\n   411→\t}\n   412→\treturn kerrors.IsInternalError(err) ||\n   413→\t\tkerrors.IsInvalid(err) ||\n   414→\t\tkerrors.IsTooManyRequests(err) ||\n   415→\t\tkerrors.IsServerTimeout(err) ||\n   416→\t\tkerrors.IsServiceUnavailable(err) ||\n   417→\t\tkerrors.IsTimeout(err) ||\n   418→\t\tkerrors.IsUnexpectedObjectError(err) ||\n   419→\t\tkerrors.IsUnexpectedServerError(err) ||\n   420→\t\tisResourceQuotaConflictErr(err) ||\n   421→\t\tisTransientNetworkErr(err) ||\n   422→\t\tisExceededQuotaErr(err) ||\n   423→\t\tisHTTP2GoawayErr(err) ||\n   424→\t\terrors.Is(err, syscall.ECONNRESET)\n   425→}\n   426→\n   427→func isHTTP2GoawayErr(err error) bool {\n   428→\treturn strings.Contains(err.Error(), \"http2: server sent GOAWAY and closed the connection\")\n   429→}\n   430→\n   431→func isExceededQuotaErr(err error) bool {\n   432→\treturn kerrors.IsForbidden(err) && strings.Contains(err.Error(), \"exceeded quota\")\n   433→}\n   434→\n   435→func isResourceQuotaConflictErr(err error) bool {\n   436→\treturn kerrors.IsConflict(err) && strings.Contains(err.Error(), \"Operation cannot be fulfilled on resourcequota\")\n   437→}\n   438→\n   439→func isTransientNetworkErr(err error) bool {\n   440→\tvar netErr net.Error\n   441→\tswitch {\n   442→\tcase errors.As(err, &netErr):\n   443→\t\tvar dnsErr *net.DNSError\n   444→\t\tvar opErr *net.OpError\n   445→\t\tvar unknownNetworkErr net.UnknownNetworkError\n   446→\t\tvar urlErr *url.Error\n   447→\t\tswitch {\n   448→\t\tcase errors.As(err, &dnsErr), errors.As(err, &opErr), errors.As(err, &unknownNetworkErr):\n   449→\t\t\treturn true\n   450→\t\tcase errors.As(err, &urlErr):\n   451→\t\t\t// For a URL error, where it replies \"connection closed\"\n   452→\t\t\t// retry again.\n   453→\t\t\treturn strings.Contains(err.Error(), \"Connection closed by foreign host\")\n   454→\t\t}\n   455→\t}\n   456→\n   457→\terrorString := err.Error()\n   458→\tvar exitErr *exec.ExitError\n   459→\tif errors.As(err, &exitErr) {\n   460→\t\terrorString = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s\", errorString, exitErr.Stderr)\n   461→\t}\n   462→\tif strings.Contains(errorString, \"net/http: TLS handshake timeout\") ||\n   463→\t\tstrings.Contains(errorString, \"i/o timeout\") ||\n   464→\t\tstrings.Contains(errorString, \"connection timed out\") ||\n   465→\t\tstrings.Contains(errorString, \"connection reset by peer\") {\n   466→\t\treturn true\n   467→\t}\n   468→\treturn false\n   469→}\n   470→\n   471→func (c *liveStateCache) getCluster(server string) (clustercache.ClusterCache, error) {\n   472→\tc.lock.RLock()\n   473→\tclusterCache, ok := c.clusters[server]\n   474→\tcacheSettings := c.cacheSettings\n   475→\tc.lock.RUnlock()\n   476→\n   477→\tif ok {\n   478→\t\treturn clusterCache, nil\n   479→\t}\n   480→\n   481→\tc.lock.Lock()\n   482→\tdefer c.lock.Unlock()\n   483→\n   484→\tclusterCache, ok = c.clusters[server]\n   485→\tif ok {\n   486→\t\treturn clusterCache, nil\n   487→\t}\n   488→\n   489→\tcluster, err := c.db.GetCluster(context.Background(), server)\n   490→\tif err != nil {\n   491→\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error getting cluster: %w\", err)\n   492→\t}\n   493→\n   494→\tif c.clusterSharding == nil {\n   495→\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"unable to handle cluster %s: cluster sharding is not configured\", cluster.Server)\n   496→\t}\n   497→\n   498→\tif !c.canHandleCluster(cluster) {\n   499→\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"controller is configured to ignore cluster %s\", cluster.Server)\n   500→\t}\n   501→\n   502→\tresourceCustomLabels, err := c.settingsMgr.GetResourceCustomLabels()\n   503→\tif err != nil {\n   504→\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error getting custom label: %w\", err)\n   505→\t}\n   506→\n   507→\trespectRBAC, err := c.settingsMgr.RespectRBAC()\n   508→\tif err != nil {\n   509→\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error getting value for %v: %w\", settings.RespectRBAC, err)\n   510→\t}\n   511→\n   512→\tclusterCacheConfig, err := cluster.RESTConfig()\n   513→\tif err != nil {\n   514→\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error getting cluster RESTConfig: %w\", err)\n   515→\t}\n   516→\t// Controller dynamically fetches all resource types available on the cluster\n   517→\t// using a discovery API that may contain deprecated APIs.\n   518→\t// This causes log flooding when managing a large number of clusters.\n   519→\t// https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/11973\n   520→\t// However, we can safely suppress deprecation warnings\n   521→\t// because we do not rely on resources with a particular API group or version.\n   522→\t// https://kubernetes.io/blog/2020/09/03/warnings/#customize-client-handling\n   523→\t//\n   524→\t// Completely suppress warning logs only for log levels that are less than Debug.\n   525→\tif log.GetLevel() < log.DebugLevel {\n   526→\t\tclusterCacheConfig.WarningHandler = rest.NoWarnings{}\n   527→\t}\n   528→\n   529→\tclusterCacheOpts := []clustercache.UpdateSettingsFunc{\n   530→\t\tclustercache.SetListSemaphore(semaphore.NewWeighted(clusterCacheListSemaphoreSize)),\n   531→\t\tclustercache.SetListPageSize(clusterCacheListPageSize),\n   532→\t\tclustercache.SetListPageBufferSize(clusterCacheListPageBufferSize),\n   533→\t\tclustercache.SetWatchResyncTimeout(clusterCacheWatchResyncDuration),\n   534→\t\tclustercache.SetClusterSyncRetryTimeout(clusterSyncRetryTimeoutDuration),\n   535→\t\tclustercache.SetResyncTimeout(clusterCacheResyncDuration),\n   536→\t\tclustercache.SetSettings(cacheSettings.clusterSettings),\n   537→\t\tclustercache.SetNamespaces(cluster.Namespaces),\n   538→\t\tclustercache.SetClusterResources(cluster.ClusterResources),\n   539→\t\tclustercache.SetPopulateResourceInfoHandler(func(un *unstructured.Unstructured, isRoot bool) (interface{}, bool) {\n   540→\t\t\tres := &ResourceInfo{}\n   541→\t\t\tpopulateNodeInfo(un, res, resourceCustomLabels)\n   542→\t\t\tc.lock.RLock()\n   543→\t\t\tcacheSettings := c.cacheSettings\n   544→\t\t\tc.lock.RUnlock()\n   545→\n   546→\t\t\tres.Health, _ = health.GetResourceHealth(un, cacheSettings.clusterSettings.ResourceHealthOverride)\n   547→\n   548→\t\t\tappName := c.resourceTracking.GetAppName(un, cacheSettings.appInstanceLabelKey, cacheSettings.trackingMethod, cacheSettings.installationID)\n   549→\t\t\tif isRoot && appName != \"\" {\n   550→\t\t\t\tres.AppName = appName\n   551→\t\t\t}\n   552→\n   553→\t\t\tgvk := un.GroupVersionKind()\n   554→\n   555→\t\t\tif cacheSettings.ignoreResourceUpdatesEnabled && shouldHashManifest(appName, gvk, un) {\n   556→\t\t\t\thash, err := generateManifestHash(un, nil, cacheSettings.resourceOverrides, c.ignoreNormalizerOpts)\n   557→\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   558→\t\t\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"Failed to generate manifest hash: %v\", err)\n   559→\t\t\t\t} else {\n   560→\t\t\t\t\tres.manifestHash = hash\n   561→\t\t\t\t}\n   562→\t\t\t}\n   563→\n   564→\t\t\t// edge case. we do not label CRDs, so they miss the tracking label we inject. But we still\n   565→\t\t\t// want the full resource to be available in our cache (to diff), so we store all CRDs\n   566→\t\t\treturn res, res.AppName != \"\" || gvk.Kind == kube.CustomResourceDefinitionKind\n   567→\t\t}),\n   568→\t\tclustercache.SetLogr(logutils.NewLogrusLogger(log.WithField(\"server\", cluster.Server))),\n   569→\t\tclustercache.SetRetryOptions(clusterCacheAttemptLimit, clusterCacheRetryUseBackoff, isRetryableError),\n   570→\t\tclustercache.SetRespectRBAC(respectRBAC),\n   571→\t\tclustercache.SetBatchEventsProcessing(clusterCacheBatchEventsProcessing),\n   572→\t\tclustercache.SetEventProcessingInterval(clusterCacheEventsProcessingInterval),\n   573→\t}\n   574→\n   575→\tclusterCache = clustercache.NewClusterCache(clusterCacheConfig, clusterCacheOpts...)\n   576→\n   577→\t_ = clusterCache.OnResourceUpdated(func(newRes *clustercache.Resource, oldRes *clustercache.Resource, namespaceResources map[kube.ResourceKey]*clustercache.Resource) {\n   578→\t\ttoNotify := make(map[string]bool)\n   579→\t\tvar ref v1.ObjectReference\n   580→\t\tif newRes != nil {\n   581→\t\t\tref = newRes.Ref\n   582→\t\t} else {\n   583→\t\t\tref = oldRes.Ref\n   584→\t\t}\n   585→\n   586→\t\tc.lock.RLock()\n   587→\t\tcacheSettings := c.cacheSettings\n   588→\t\tc.lock.RUnlock()\n   589→\n   590→\t\tif cacheSettings.ignoreResourceUpdatesEnabled && oldRes != nil && newRes != nil && skipResourceUpdate(resInfo(oldRes), resInfo(newRes)) {\n   591→\t\t\t// Additional check for debug level so we don't need to evaluate the\n   592→\t\t\t// format string in case of non-debug scenarios\n   593→\t\t\tif log.GetLevel() >= log.DebugLevel {\n   594→\t\t\t\tnamespace := ref.Namespace\n   595→\t\t\t\tif ref.Namespace == \"\" {\n   596→\t\t\t\t\tnamespace = \"(cluster-scoped)\"\n   597→\t\t\t\t}\n   598→\t\t\t\tlog.WithFields(log.Fields{\n   599→\t\t\t\t\t\"server\":      cluster.Server,\n   600→\t\t\t\t\t\"namespace\":   namespace,\n   601→\t\t\t\t\t\"name\":        ref.Name,\n   602→\t\t\t\t\t\"api-version\": ref.APIVersion,\n   603→\t\t\t\t\t\"kind\":        ref.Kind,\n   604→\t\t\t\t}).Debug(\"Ignoring change of object because none of the watched resource fields have changed\")\n   605→\t\t\t}\n   606→\t\t\treturn\n   607→\t\t}\n   608→\n   609→\t\tfor _, r := range []*clustercache.Resource{newRes, oldRes} {\n   610→\t\t\tif r == nil {\n   611→\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   612→\t\t\t}\n   613→\t\t\tapp := getApp(r, namespaceResources)\n   614→\t\t\tif app == \"\" || skipAppRequeuing(r.ResourceKey()) {\n   615→\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   616→\t\t\t}\n   617→\t\t\ttoNotify[app] = isRootAppNode(r) || toNotify[app]\n   618→\t\t}\n   619→\t\tc.onObjectUpdated(toNotify, ref)\n   620→\t})\n   621→\n   622→\t_ = clusterCache.OnEvent(func(event watch.EventType, un *unstructured.Unstructured) {\n   623→\t\tgvk := un.GroupVersionKind()\n   624→\t\tc.metricsServer.IncClusterEventsCount(cluster.Server, gvk.Group, gvk.Kind)\n   625→\t})\n   626→\n   627→\t_ = clusterCache.OnProcessEventsHandler(func(duration time.Duration, processedEventsNumber int) {\n   628→\t\tc.metricsServer.ObserveResourceEventsProcessingDuration(cluster.Server, duration, processedEventsNumber)\n   629→\t})\n   630→\n   631→\tc.clusters[server] = clusterCache\n   632→\n   633→\treturn clusterCache, nil\n   634→}\n   635→\n   636→func (c *liveStateCache) getSyncedCluster(server string) (clustercache.ClusterCache, error) {\n   637→\tclusterCache, err := c.getCluster(server)\n   638→\tif err != nil {\n   639→\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error getting cluster: %w\", err)\n   640→\t}\n   641→\terr = clusterCache.EnsureSynced()\n   642→\tif err != nil {\n   643→\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error synchronizing cache state : %w\", err)\n   644→\t}\n   645→\treturn clusterCache, nil\n   646→}\n   647→\n   648→func (c *liveStateCache) invalidate(cacheSettings cacheSettings) {\n   649→\tlog.Info(\"invalidating live state cache\")\n   650→\tc.lock.Lock()\n   651→\tc.cacheSettings = cacheSettings\n   652→\tclusters := c.clusters\n   653→\tc.lock.Unlock()\n   654→\n   655→\tfor _, clust := range clusters {\n   656→\t\tclust.Invalidate(clustercache.SetSettings(cacheSettings.clusterSettings))\n   657→\t}\n   658→\tlog.Info(\"live state cache invalidated\")\n   659→}\n   660→\n   661→func (c *liveStateCache) IsNamespaced(server string, gk schema.GroupKind) (bool, error) {\n   662→\tclusterInfo, err := c.getSyncedCluster(server)\n   663→\tif err != nil {\n   664→\t\treturn false, err\n   665→\t}\n   666→\treturn clusterInfo.IsNamespaced(gk)\n   667→}\n   668→\n   669→func (c *liveStateCache) IterateHierarchy(server string, key kube.ResourceKey, action func(child appv1.ResourceNode, appName string) bool) error {\n   670→\tclusterInfo, err := c.getSyncedCluster(server)\n   671→\tif err != nil {\n   672→\t\treturn err\n   673→\t}\n   674→\tclusterInfo.IterateHierarchy(key, func(resource *clustercache.Resource, namespaceResources map[kube.ResourceKey]*clustercache.Resource) bool {\n   675→\t\treturn action(asResourceNode(resource), getApp(resource, namespaceResources))\n   676→\t})\n   677→\treturn nil\n   678→}\n   679→\n   680→func (c *liveStateCache) IterateHierarchyV2(server string, keys []kube.ResourceKey, action func(child appv1.ResourceNode, appName string) bool) error {\n   681→\tclusterInfo, err := c.getSyncedCluster(server)\n   682→\tif err != nil {\n   683→\t\treturn err\n   684→\t}\n   685→\tclusterInfo.IterateHierarchyV2(keys, func(resource *clustercache.Resource, namespaceResources map[kube.ResourceKey]*clustercache.Resource) bool {\n   686→\t\treturn action(asResourceNode(resource), getApp(resource, namespaceResources))\n   687→\t})\n   688→\treturn nil\n   689→}\n   690→\n   691→func (c *liveStateCache) IterateResources(server string, callback func(res *clustercache.Resource, info *ResourceInfo)) error {\n   692→\tclusterInfo, err := c.getSyncedCluster(server)\n   693→\tif err != nil {\n   694→\t\treturn err\n   695→\t}\n   696→\t_ = clusterInfo.FindResources(\"\", func(r *clustercache.Resource) bool {\n   697→\t\tif info, ok := r.Info.(*ResourceInfo); ok {\n   698→\t\t\tcallback(r, info)\n   699→\t\t}\n   700→\t\treturn false\n   701→\t})\n   702→\treturn nil\n   703→}\n   704→\n   705→func (c *liveStateCache) GetNamespaceTopLevelResources(server string, namespace string) (map[kube.ResourceKey]appv1.ResourceNode, error) {\n   706→\tclusterInfo, err := c.getSyncedCluster(server)\n   707→\tif err != nil {\n   708→\t\treturn nil, err\n   709→\t}\n   710→\tresources := clusterInfo.FindResources(namespace, clustercache.TopLevelResource)\n   711→\tres := make(map[kube.ResourceKey]appv1.ResourceNode)\n   712→\tfor k, r := range resources {\n   713→\t\tres[k] = asResourceNode(r)\n   714→\t}\n   715→\treturn res, nil\n   716→}\n   717→\n   718→func (c *liveStateCache) GetManagedLiveObjs(a *appv1.Application, targetObjs []*unstructured.Unstructured) (map[kube.ResourceKey]*unstructured.Unstructured, error) {\n   719→\tclusterInfo, err := c.getSyncedCluster(a.Spec.Destination.Server)\n   720→\tif err != nil {\n   721→\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get cluster info for %q: %w\", a.Spec.Destination.Server, err)\n   722→\t}\n   723→\treturn clusterInfo.GetManagedLiveObjs(targetObjs, func(r *clustercache.Resource) bool {\n   724→\t\treturn resInfo(r).AppName == a.InstanceName(c.settingsMgr.GetNamespace())\n   725→\t})\n   726→}\n   727→\n   728→func (c *liveStateCache) GetVersionsInfo(serverURL string) (string, []kube.APIResourceInfo, error) {\n   729→\tclusterInfo, err := c.getSyncedCluster(serverURL)\n   730→\tif err != nil {\n   731→\t\treturn \"\", nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get cluster info for %q: %w\", serverURL, err)\n   732→\t}\n   733→\treturn clusterInfo.GetServerVersion(), clusterInfo.GetAPIResources(), nil\n   734→}\n   735→\n   736→func (c *liveStateCache) isClusterHasApps(apps []interface{}, cluster *appv1.Cluster) bool {\n   737→\tfor _, obj := range apps {\n   738→\t\tapp, ok := obj.(*appv1.Application)\n   739→\t\tif !ok {\n   740→\t\t\tcontinue\n   741→\t\t}\n   742→\t\terr := argo.ValidateDestination(context.Background(), &app.Spec.Destination, c.db)\n   743→\t\tif err != nil {\n   744→\t\t\tcontinue\n   745→\t\t}\n   746→\t\tif app.Spec.Destination.Server == cluster.Server {\n   747→\t\t\treturn true\n   748→\t\t}\n   749→\t}\n   750→\treturn false\n   751→}\n   752→\n   753→func (c *liveStateCache) watchSettings(ctx context.Context) {\n   754→\tupdateCh := make(chan *settings.ArgoCDSettings, 1)\n   755→\tc.settingsMgr.Subscribe(updateCh)\n   756→\n   757→\tdone := false\n   758→\tfor !done {\n   759→\t\tselect {\n   760→\t\tcase <-updateCh:\n   761→\t\t\tnextCacheSettings, err := c.loadCacheSettings()\n   762→\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   763→\t\t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Failed to read updated settings: %v\", err)\n   764→\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   765→\t\t\t}\n   766→\n   767→\t\t\tc.lock.Lock()\n   768→\t\t\tneedInvalidate := false\n   769→\t\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(c.cacheSettings, *nextCacheSettings) {\n   770→\t\t\t\tc.cacheSettings = *nextCacheSettings\n   771→\t\t\t\tneedInvalidate = true\n   772→\t\t\t}\n   773→\t\t\tc.lock.Unlock()\n   774→\t\t\tif needInvalidate {\n   775→\t\t\t\tc.invalidate(*nextCacheSettings)\n   776→\t\t\t}\n   777→\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n   778→\t\t\tdone = true\n   779→\t\t}\n   780→\t}\n   781→\tlog.Info(\"shutting down settings watch\")\n   782→\tc.settingsMgr.Unsubscribe(updateCh)\n   783→\tclose(updateCh)\n   784→}\n   785→\n   786→func (c *liveStateCache) Init() error {\n   787→\tcacheSettings, err := c.loadCacheSettings()\n   788→\tif err != nil {\n   789→\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error loading cache settings: %w\", err)\n   790→\t}\n   791→\tc.cacheSettings = *cacheSettings\n   792→\treturn nil\n   793→}\n   794→\n   795→// Run watches for resource changes annotated with application label on all registered clusters and schedule corresponding app refresh.\n   796→func (c *liveStateCache) Run(ctx context.Context) error {\n   797→\tgo c.watchSettings(ctx)\n   798→\n   799→\tkube.RetryUntilSucceed(ctx, clustercache.ClusterRetryTimeout, \"watch clusters\", logutils.NewLogrusLogger(logutils.NewWithCurrentConfig()), func() error {\n   800→\t\treturn c.db.WatchClusters(ctx, c.handleAddEvent, c.handleModEvent, c.handleDeleteEvent)\n   801→\t})\n   802→\n   803→\t<-ctx.Done()\n   804→\tc.invalidate(c.cacheSettings)\n   805→\treturn nil\n   806→}\n   807→\n   808→func (c *liveStateCache) canHandleCluster(cluster *appv1.Cluster) bool {\n   809→\treturn c.clusterSharding.IsManagedCluster(cluster)\n   810→}\n   811→\n   812→func (c *liveStateCache) handleAddEvent(cluster *appv1.Cluster) {\n   813→\tc.clusterSharding.Add(cluster)\n   814→\tif !c.canHandleCluster(cluster) {\n   815→\t\tlog.Infof(\"Ignoring cluster %s\", cluster.Server)\n   816→\t\treturn\n   817→\t}\n   818→\tc.lock.Lock()\n   819→\t_, ok := c.clusters[cluster.Server]\n   820→\tc.lock.Unlock()\n   821→\tif !ok {\n   822→\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Checking if cache %v / cluster %v has appInformer %v\", c, cluster, c.appInformer)\n   823→\t\tif c.appInformer == nil {\n   824→\t\t\tlog.Warn(\"Cannot get a cluster appInformer. Cache may not be started this time\")\n   825→\t\t\treturn\n   826→\t\t}\n   827→\t\tif c.isClusterHasApps(c.appInformer.GetStore().List(), cluster) {\n   828→\t\t\tgo func() {\n   829→\t\t\t\t// warm up cache for cluster with apps\n   830→\t\t\t\t_, _ = c.getSyncedCluster(cluster.Server)\n   831→\t\t\t}()\n   832→\t\t}\n   833→\t}\n   834→}\n   835→\n   836→func (c *liveStateCache) handleModEvent(oldCluster *appv1.Cluster, newCluster *appv1.Cluster) {\n   837→\tc.clusterSharding.Update(oldCluster, newCluster)\n   838→\tc.lock.Lock()\n   839→\tcluster, ok := c.clusters[newCluster.Server]\n   840→\tc.lock.Unlock()\n   841→\tif ok {\n   842→\t\tif !c.canHandleCluster(newCluster) {\n   843→\t\t\tcluster.Invalidate()\n   844→\t\t\tc.lock.Lock()\n   845→\t\t\tdelete(c.clusters, newCluster.Server)\n   846→\t\t\tc.lock.Unlock()\n   847→\t\t\treturn\n   848→\t\t}\n   849→\n   850→\t\tvar updateSettings []clustercache.UpdateSettingsFunc\n   851→\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(oldCluster.Config, newCluster.Config) {\n   852→\t\t\tnewClusterRESTConfig, err := newCluster.RESTConfig()\n   853→\t\t\tif err == nil {\n   854→\t\t\t\tupdateSettings = append(updateSettings, clustercache.SetConfig(newClusterRESTConfig))\n   855→\t\t\t} else {\n   856→\t\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"error getting cluster REST config: %v\", err)\n   857→\t\t\t}\n   858→\t\t}\n   859→\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(oldCluster.Namespaces, newCluster.Namespaces) {\n   860→\t\t\tupdateSettings = append(updateSettings, clustercache.SetNamespaces(newCluster.Namespaces))\n   861→\t\t}\n   862→\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(oldCluster.ClusterResources, newCluster.ClusterResources) {\n   863→\t\t\tupdateSettings = append(updateSettings, clustercache.SetClusterResources(newCluster.ClusterResources))\n   864→\t\t}\n   865→\t\tforceInvalidate := false\n   866→\t\tif newCluster.RefreshRequestedAt != nil &&\n {\n   413→\tif diffConfig == nil {\n   414→\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"preDiffNormalize error: diffConfig can not be nil\")\n   415→\t}\n   416→\terr := diffConfig.Validate()\n   417→\tif err != nil 432→\t\t\tok, ignoreDiff := idc.HasIgnoreDifference(gvk.Group, gvk.Kind, target.GetName(), target.GetNamespace())\n   433→\t\t\tif ok && len(ignoreDiff.ManagedFieldsManagers) > 0 {\n   434→\t\t\t\tpt Let me create a comprehensive report of my findings:\n\n## Argo CD Diff Computation Architecture - Comprehensive Investigation\n\nBased on my analysis of the Argo CD codebase, here's a detailed explanation of how Argo CD computes diffs between desired (Git) and live (cluster) state:\n\n---\n\n### 1. **Component Fetching Live State from the Cluster**\n\n**File: `/workspace/controller/cache/cache.go`**\n\nThe **LiveStateCache** interface is the primary component responsible for fetching live state:\n\n```go\ntype LiveStateCache interface {\n    GetVersionsInfo(serverURL string) (string, []kube.APIResourceInfo, error)\n    IsNamespaced(server string, gk schema.GroupKind) (bool, error)\n    GetClusterCache(server string) (clustercache.ClusterCache, error)\n    GetManagedLiveObjs(a *appv1.Application, targetObjs []*unstructured.Unstructured) \n        (map[kube.ResourceKey]*unstructured.Unstructured, error)\n    // ... other methods\n}\n```\n\n**Key Implementation Details:**\n- Built on `clustercache.ClusterCache` from gitops-engine\n- Maintains watch on all cluster resources using Kubernetes informers\n- Automatically syncs cluster state with configurable resync intervals (default: 12 hours)\n- Caches resources with metadata including health status, networking info, and pod info\n- Filters resources by namespace and cluster-scoped resources\n- Tracks app ownership using resource tracking labels/annotations\n\n**Entry Point in CompareAppState (line 611):**\n```go\nliveObjByKey, err := m.liveStateCache.GetManagedLiveObjs(app, targetObjs)\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. **Resource Normalization Before Comparison**\n\nNormalization happens in **two stages** before diff calculation:\n\n#### **Stage 1: Pre-Diff Normalization** (`/workspace/util/argo/diff/diff.go`)\n\n**Function: `preDiffNormalize()` (lines 412-446)**\n\n```go\nfunc preDiffNormalize(lives, targets []*unstructured.Unstructured, diffConfig DiffConfig) \n    (*NormalizationResult, error) {\n    // Step 1: Resource Tracking Normalization\n    resourceTracking := argo.NewResourceTracking()\n    _ = resourceTracking.Normalize(target, live, diffConfig.AppLabelKey(), diffConfig.TrackingMethod())\n    \n    // Step 2: Managed Fields Normalization (if applicable)\n    if live != nil && target != nil {\n        gvk := target.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind()\n        idc := NewIgnoreDiffConfig(diffConfig.Ignores(), diffConfig.Overrides())\n        ok, ignoreDiff := idc.HasIgnoreDifference(gvk.Group, gvk.Kind, ...)\n        if ok && len(ignoreDiff.ManagedFieldsManagers) > 0 {\n            live, target, err = managedfields.Normalize(live, target, \n                ignoreDiff.ManagedFieldsManagers, pt)\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Purpose:**\n- Removes/normalizes resource tracking labels and annotations that Argo CD adds\n- Handles managed fields by giving precedence to trusted managers (e.g., controllers)\n- Deep copies resources to avoid mutations\n\n#### **Stage 2: Full Diff Normalization** (`/workspace/util/argo/diff/normalize.go`)\n\n**Composable Normalizer Pattern:**\n```go\ntype composableNormalizer struct {\n    normalizers []diff.Normalizer\n}\n```\n\n**Two Normalizers Applied:**\n\n1. **IgnoreNormalizer** - removes fields based on:\n   - JSONPointers (RFC 6902 JSON Patch format)\n   - JQPathExpressions (JQ query language)\n   - ManagedFieldsManagers (from ResourceIgnoreDifferences)\n\n2. **KnownTypesNormalizer** - re-formats custom Kubernetes fields:\n   - Parses Kubernetes types like `core/Quantity`, `meta/v1/Duration`\n   - Handles resource field parsing (from ResourceOverride)\n   - Ensures type consistency between live and desired states\n\n**Application in CompareAppState (line 721):**\n```go\ndiffConfigBuilder := argodiff.NewDiffConfigBuilder().\n    WithDiffSettings(app.Spec.IgnoreDifferences, resourceOverrides, \n        compareOptions.IgnoreAggregatedRoles, m.ignoreNormalizerOpts)\n```\n\n---\n\n### 3. **Different Diff Strategies and When They're Used**\n\nArgo CD supports **three distinct diff strategies**, selected based on configuration:\n\n#### **Strategy A: Standard 3-Way Merge Diff (Default)**\n- Uses kubectl's built-in 3-way merge algorithm\n- Compares: live state, desired state, and last applied state\n- Works with most Kubernetes resources\n- **When Used:** Default behavior, most resources\n\n#### **Strategy B: Structured Merge Diff**\n- Uses Kubernetes' structured merge patch (Server-Side Apply compatible)\n- Tracks field ownership and applies without conflicts\n- **When Used:** \n  - Application has `SyncPolicy.SyncOptions.ServerSideApply=true` (line 754)\n  - Enables GVKParser and Manager configuration\n\n**Code Reference (line 754-756):**\n```go\nif app.Spec.SyncPolicy != nil && app.Spec.SyncPolicy.SyncOptions.HasOption(\"ServerSideApply=true\") {\n    diffConfigBuilder.WithStructuredMergeDiff(true)\n}\n```\n\n#### **Strategy C: Server-Side Diff**\n- Dry-runs apply on the actual server\n- Detects real-world conflicts before syncing\n- More accurate but slower and more API-intensive\n- **When Used:**\n  - Enabled globally: `--server-side-diff` flag or env var\n  - Per-app override: `argocd.argoproj.io/compare-result: ServerSideDiff=true` annotation\n  - Disabled per-app: `ServerSideDiff=false` annotation takes precedence (line 714-715)\n\n**Code Reference (line 709-751):**\n```go\nserverSideDiff := m.serverSideDiff ||\n    resourceutil.HasAnnotationOption(app, common.AnnotationCompareOptions, \"ServerSideDiff=true\")\n\nif resourceutil.HasAnnotationOption(app, common.AnnotationCompareOptions, \"ServerSideDiff=false\") {\n    serverSideDiff = false\n}\n\nif serverSideDiff {\n    applier, cleanup, err := m.getServerSideDiffDryRunApplier(app.Spec.Destination.Server)\n    defer cleanup()\n    diffConfigBuilder.WithServerSideDryRunner(diff.NewK8sServerSideDryRunner(applier))\n}\n```\n\n#### **Special Options:**\n- **IgnoreAggregatedRoles:** Ignores changes to aggregated RBAC roles (line 312)\n- **IgnoreMutationWebhook:** Controls whether to include webhook mutations in diff (line 318)\n\n---\n\n### 4. **Diff Result Representation and Out-of-Sync Indication**\n\n#### **Diff Result Structure** (from gitops-engine)\n\n```go\ntype DiffResult struct {\n    Modified         bool     // true if live differs from desired\n    NormalizedLive   []byte   // normalized live state as JSON\n    PredictedLive    []byte   // what live state will be after apply\n}\n\ntype DiffResultList struct {\n    Diffs    []DiffResult\n    Modified bool\n}\n```\n\n#### **Sync Status Determination** (`/workspace/pkg/apis/application/v1alpha1/types.go`)\n\n```go\ntype SyncStatusCode string\n\nconst (\n    SyncStatusCodeUnknown   SyncStatusCode = \"Unknown\"   // Status unknown (errors during comparison)\n    SyncStatusCodeSynced    SyncStatusCode = \"Synced\"    // Desired == Live\n    SyncStatusCodeOutOfSync SyncStatusCode = \"OutOfSync\" // Desired != Live\n)\n\ntype SyncStatus struct {\n    Status         SyncStatusCode     // Overall sync status\n    ComparedTo     ComparedTo         // What was compared\n    Revision       string             // Git revision (single source)\n    Revisions      []string           // Git revisions (multi-source)\n}\n\ntype ResourceStatus struct {\n    Group                    string         // Resource GVK\n    Kind                     string\n    Version                  string\n    Name                     string         // Resource name\n    Namespace                string\n    Status                   SyncStatusCode // Individual resource sync status\n    Health                   *HealthStatus  // Health status\n    RequiresPruning          bool           // Resource exists in cluster but not in Git\n    RequiresDeletionConfirm  bool           // Deletion requires confirmation\n}\n```\n\n#### **Sync Status Decision Logic** (lines 823-875 in `/workspace/controller/state.go`)\n\n```go\n// Overall application status is OutOfSync if ANY resource is modified\nif !isManagedNs && (diffResult.Modified || targetObj == nil || liveObj == nil) {\n    // Set resource state to OutOfSync since one of the following is true:\n    // * target and live resource are different\n    // * target resource not defined and live resource is extra (requires pruning)\n    // * target resource present but live resource is missing\n    resState.Status = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeOutOfSync\n    needsPruning := targetObj == nil && liveObj != nil\n    if !(needsPruning && resourceutil.HasAnnotationOption(obj, common.AnnotationCompareOptions, \"IgnoreExtraneous\")) {\n        syncCode = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeOutOfSync\n    }\n} else {\n    resState.Status = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeSynced\n}\n\n// Set Unknown if unable to load objects\nif failedToLoadObjs {\n    syncCode = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeUnknown\n}\n```\n\n#### **Out-of-Sync Conditions:**\n\nA resource is marked **OutOfSync** if:\n\n1. **`diffResult.Modified == true`** - Live state differs from desired after normalization\n2. **`targetObj == nil && liveObj != nil`** - Extra resource in cluster (pruning needed)\n3. **`targetObj != nil && liveObj == nil`** - Missing resource on cluster (needs sync)\n4. **`failedToLoadObjs == true`** - Comparison failed → Status set to Unknown\n5. **`app.HasChangedManagedNamespaceMetadata()`** - Managed namespace metadata changed\n\n#### **Special Cases:**\n\n- **Managed Namespaces** with `managedNamespaceMetadata` are excluded from out-of-sync check\n- **Resource Hooks** and **Skipped Resources** don't affect overall sync status\n- **Shared Resources** generate warnings but can still sync\n\n---\n\n### **Summary Flow Diagram**\n\n```\nApplication Spec (Git)\n        ↓\n    Repo Server\n    (Manifest Generation)\n        ↓\n  Target Objects (YAML)\n        ↓\n   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐\n   │  CompareAppState()                      │\n   │  =====================                 │\n   │  1. GetRepoObjs() → targetObjs          │\n   │  2. GetManagedLiveObjs() → liveObjs     │\n   │  3. sync.Reconcile() → pair objects     │\n   │  4. Build DiffConfig with:              │\n   │     - Ignore rules                      │\n   │     - Normalizations                    │\n   │     - Diff strategy (SSD, SMD, 3-way)  │\n   │  5. StateDiffs(live, target, diffCfg)  │\n   │     └─ Pre-normalize                    │\n   │     └─ Apply Normalizers                │\n   │     └─ Diff strategy selected           │\n   │  6. Generate ResourceStatus per diff    │\n   │  7. Determine overall SyncStatus        │\n   └─────────────────────────────────────────┘\n        ↓\nCluster State (Live Objects)\n   LiveStateCache\n   (from cluster watches)\n        ↓\n    DiffResults\n   (Modified: bool)\n        ↓\n  ResourceStatus[]\n   (per resource)\n        ↓\n   SyncStatus\n  (Synced/OutOfSync/Unknown)\n```\n\n---\n\n### **Configuration Points**\n\n**Application Level:**\n- `spec.ignoreDifferences` - Which fields/resources to ignore\n- `spec.syncPolicy.syncOptions` - ServerSideApply, IgnoreExtraneous, etc.\n- Annotations: `argocd.argoproj.io/compare-result`\n\n**System Level:**\n- `argocd-cm` ConfigMap: `resource.ignoreResourceUpdates.enabled`, `ignore.rules`\n- `argocd-cmd-params-cm`: Resource overrides, known types configuration\n- CLI/Env: `--server-side-diff`, ignore normalizer options\n\nThis architecture enables Argo CD to accurately detect configuration drift while being flexible enough to handle complex Kubernetes scenarios with custom controllers and field managers."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: ace5e85e7482b351b (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)\n<usage>total_tokens: 70712\ntool_uses: 20\nduration_ms: 52444</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"61763242-f70f-4cb0-a3b8-fd590c2f9939","uuid":"0c4c5429-b7c5-46fc-9255-9e10495941d6","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Investigate how Argo CD computes diffs between desired (Git) and live (cluster) state.\n\nSpecific questions:\n1. []*unstructured.Unstructured, diffConfig DiffConfig) \n    (*NormalizationResult, error) {\n    // Step 1: Resource Tracking Normalization\n    resourceTracking := argo.NewResourceTracking()\n    _ = {\n        gvk := target.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind()\n        idc := NewIgnoreDiffConfig(diffConfig.Ignores(), diffConfig.Overrides())\n        ok, ignoreDiff := idc.HasIgnoreDifference(gvk.Group, gvk.Kind, ...)\n        if ok && len(ignoreDiff.ManagedFieldsManagers) > 0 {\n            live, target, err = managedfields.Normalize(live, target, \n                ignoreDiff.ManagedFieldsManagers, pt)\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Purpose:**\n- Removes/normalizes resource tracking labels and annotations that Argo CD adds\n- Handles managed fields by giving precedence to trusted managers (e.g., controllers)\n- Deep copies resources to avoid mutations\n\n#### **Stage 2: Full Diff Normalization** (`/workspace/util/argo/diff/normalize.go`)\n\n**Composable Normalizer Pattern:**\n```go\ntype composableNormalizer struct {\n    normalizers []diff.Normalizer\n}\n```\n\n**Two Normalizers Applied:**\n\n1. **IgnoreNormalizer** - removes fields based on:\n   - JSONPointers (RFC 6902 JSON Patch format)\n   - JQPathExpressions (JQ query language)\n   - ManagedFieldsManagers (from ResourceIgnoreDifferences)\n\n2. **KnownTypesNormalizer** - re-formats custom Kubernetes fields:\n   - Parses Kubernetes types like `core/Quantity`, `meta/v1/Duration`\n   - Handles resource field parsing (from ResourceOverride)\n   - Ensures type consistency between live and desired states\n\n**Application in CompareAppState (line 721):**\n```go\ndiffConfigBuilder := argodiff.NewDiffConfigBuilder().\n    WithDiffSettings(app.Spec.IgnoreDifferences, resourceOverrides, \n        compareOptions.IgnoreAggregatedRoles, m.ignoreNormalizerOpts)\n```\n\n---\n\n### 3. **Different Diff Strategies and When They're Used**\n\nArgo CD supports **three distinct diff strategies**, selected based on configuration:\n\n#### **Strategy A: Standard 3-Way Merge Diff (Default)**\n- Uses kubectl's built-in 3-way merge algorithm\n- Compares: live state, desired state, and last applied state\n- Works with most Kubernetes resources\n- **When Used:** Default behavior, most resources\n\n#### **Strategy B: Structured Merge Diff**\n- Uses Kubernetes' structured merge patch (Server-Side Apply compatible)\n- Tracks field ownership and applies without conflicts\n- **When Used:** \n  - Application has `SyncPolicy.SyncOptions.ServerSideApply=true` (line 754)\n  - Enables GVKParser and Manager configuration\n\n**Code Reference (line 754-756):**\n```go\nif app.Spec.SyncPolicy != nil && app.Spec.SyncPolicy.SyncOptions.HasOption(\"ServerSideApply=true\") {\n    diffConfigBuilder.WithStructuredMergeDiff(true)\n}\n```\n\n#### **Strategy C: Server-Side Diff**\n- Dry-runs apply on the actual server\n- Detects real-world conflicts before syncing\n- More accurate but slower and more API-intensive\n- **When Used:**\n  - Enabled globally: `--server-side-diff` flag or env var\n  - Per-app override: `argocd.argoproj.io/compare-result: ServerSideDiff=true` annotation\n  - Disabled per-app: `ServerSideDiff=false` annotation takes precedence (line 714-715)\n\n**Code Reference (line 709-751):**\n```go\nserverSideDiff := m.serverSideDiff ||\n    resourceutil.HasAnnotationOption(app, common.AnnotationCompareOptions, \"ServerSideDiff=true\")\n\nif resourceutil.HasAnnotationOption(app, common.AnnotationCompareOptions, \"ServerSideDiff=false\") {\n    serverSideDiff = false\n}\n\nif serverSideDiff {\n    applier, cleanup, err := m.getServerSideDiffDryRunApplier(app.Spec.Destination.Server)\n    defer cleanup()\n    diffConfigBuilder.WithServerSideDryRunner(diff.NewK8sServerSideDryRunner(applier))\n}\n```\n\n#### **Special Options:**\n- **IgnoreAggregatedRoles:** Ignores changes to aggregated RBAC roles (line 312)\n- **IgnoreMutationWebhook:** Controls whether to include webhook mutations in diff (line 318)\n\n---\n\n### 4. **Diff Result Representation and Out-of-Sync Indication**\n\n#### **Diff Result Structure** (from gitops-engine)\n\n```go\ntype DiffResult struct {\n    Modified         bool     // true if live differs from desired\n    NormalizedLive   []byte   // normalized live state as JSON\n    PredictedLive    []byte   // what live state will be after apply\n}\n\ntype DiffResultList struct {\n    Diffs    []DiffResult\n    Modified bool\n}\n```\n\n#### **Sync Status Determination** (`/workspace/pkg/apis/application/v1alpha1/types.go`)\n\n```go\ntype SyncStatusCode string\n\nconst (\n    SyncStatusCodeUnknown   SyncStatusCode = \"Unknown\"   // Status unknown (errors during comparison)\n    SyncStatusCodeSynced    SyncStatusCode = \"Synced\"    // Desired == Live\n    SyncStatusCodeOutOfSync SyncStatusCode = \"OutOfSync\" // Desired != Live\n)\n\ntype SyncStatus struct {\n    Status         SyncStatusCode     // Overall sync status\n    ComparedTo     ComparedTo         // What was compared\n    Revision       string             // Git revision (single source)\n    Revisions      []string           // Git revisions (multi-source)\n}\n\ntype ResourceStatus struct {\n    Group                    string         // Resource GVK\n    Kind                     string\n    Version                  string\n    Name                     string         // Resource name\n    Namespace                string\n    Status                   SyncStatusCode // Individual resource sync status\n    Health                   *HealthStatus  // Health status\n    RequiresPruning          bool           // Resource exists in cluster but not in Git\n    RequiresDeletionConfirm  bool           // Deletion requires confirmation\n}\n```\n\n#### **Sync Status Decision Logic** (lines 823-875 in `/workspace/controller/state.go`)\n\n```go\n// Overall application status is OutOfSync if ANY resource is modified\nif !isManagedNs && (diffResult.Modified || targetObj == nil || liveObj == nil) {\n    // Set resource state to OutOfSync since one of the following is true:\n    // * target and live resource are different\n    // * target resource not defined and live resource is extra (requires pruning)\n    // * target resource present but live resource is missing\n    resState.Status = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeOutOfSync\n    needsPruning := targetObj == nil && liveObj != nil\n    if !(needsPruning && resourceutil.HasAnnotationOption(obj, common.AnnotationCompareOptions, \"IgnoreExtraneous\")) {\n        syncCode = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeOutOfSync\n    }\n} else {\n    resState.Status = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeSynced\n}\n\n// Set Unknown if unable to load objects\nif failedToLoadObjs {\n    syncCode = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeUnknown\n}\n```\n\n#### **Out-of-Sync Conditions:**\n\nA resource is marked **OutOfSync** if:\n\n1. **`diffResult.Modified == true`** - Live state differs from desired after normalization\n2. **`targetObj == nil && liveObj != nil`** - Extra resource in cluster (pruning needed)\n3. **`targetObj != nil && liveObj == nil`** - Missing resource on cluster (needs sync)\n4. **`failedToLoadObjs == true`** - Comparison failed → Status set to Unknown\n5. **`app.HasChangedManagedNamespaceMetadata()`** - Managed namespace metadata changed\n\n#### **Special Cases:**\n\n- **Managed Namespaces** with `managedNamespaceMetadata` are excluded from out-of-sync check\n- **Resource Hooks** and **Skipped Resources** don't affect overall sync status\n- **Shared Resources** generate warnings but can still sync\n\n---\n\n### **Summary Flow Diagram**\n\n```\nApplication Spec (Git)\n        ↓\n    Repo Server\n    (Manifest Generation)\n        ↓\n  Target Objects (YAML)\n        ↓\n   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐\n   │  CompareAppState()                      │\n   │  =====================                 │\n   │  1. GetRepoObjs() → targetObjs          │\n   │  2. GetManagedLiveObjs() → liveObjs     │\n   │  3. sync.Reconcile() → pair objects     │\n   │  4. Build DiffConfig with:              │\n   │     - Ignore rules                      │\n   │     - Normalizations                    │\n   │     - Diff strategy (SSD, SMD, 3-way)  │\n   │  5. StateDiffs(live, target, diffCfg)  │\n   │     └─ Pre-normalize                    │\n   │     └─ Apply Normalizers                │\n   │     └─ Diff strategy selected           │\n   │  6. Generate ResourceStatus per diff    │\n   │  7. Determine overall SyncStatus        │\n   └─────────────────────────────────────────┘\n        ↓\nCluster State (Live Objects)\n   LiveStateCache\n   (from cluster watches)\n        ↓\n    DiffResults\n   (Modified: bool)\n        ↓\n  ResourceStatus[]\n   (per resource)\n        ↓\n   SyncStatus\n  (Synced/OutOfSync/Unknown)\n```\n\n---\n\n### **Configuration Points**\n\n**Application Level:**\n- `spec.ignoreDifferences` - Which fields/resources to ignore\n- `spec.syncPolicy.syncOptions` - ServerSideApply, IgnoreExtraneous, etc.\n- Annotations: `argocd.argoproj.io/compare-result`\n\n**System Level:**\n- `argocd-cm` ConfigMap: `resource.ignoreResourceUpdates.enabled`, `ignore.rules`\n- `argocd-cmd-params-cm`: Resource overrides, known types configuration\n- CLI/Env: `--server-side-diff`, ignore normalizer options\n\nThis architecture enables Argo CD to accurately detect configuration drift while being flexible enough to handle complex Kubernetes scenarios with custom controllers and field managers."}],"totalDurationMs":52444,"totalTokens":70712,"totalToolUseCount":20,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2862,"cache_read_input_tokens":64568,"output_tokens":3280,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2862,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

 205→\tsyncRes.Revisions = compareResult.syncStatus.Revisions\n   206→\n   207→\t// validates if it should fail the sync if it finds shared resources\n   208→\thasSharedResource, sharedResourceMessage normalizeTargetResources modifies target resources to ensure ignored fields are not touched during synchronization:\n   467→//   - applies normalization to the target resources based on the live resources\n   468→//   - copies ignored fields from the matching live resources: apply normalizer to the live resource,\n   469→//     calculates the patch performed by normalizer and applies the patch to the target resource\n   470→func normalizeTargetResources(cr *comparisonResult) ([]*unstructured.Unstructured, error) {\n   471→\t// normalize live and target resources\n   472→\tnormalized, 493→\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   494→\t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   495→\t\t\t}\n   496→\t\t\tlookupPatchMeta = &meta\n   497→\t\t}\n   498→\n   499→\t\tlivePatch, err := unstructures.\n   516→func getMergePatch(original, modified *unstructured.Unstructured, lookupPatchMeta *strategicpatch.PatchMetaFromStruct) ([]byte, error) {\n   517→\toriginalJSON, err := 529→\treturn jsonpatch.CreateMergePatch(originalJSON, modifiedJSON)\n   530→}\n   531→\n   532→// applyMergePatch will apply the given patch in the obj and return the patched\n   533→// unstructure.\n   534→func applyMergePatch(obj *unstructured.Unstructured, patch []byte, versionedObject interface{}) (*unstructured.Unstructured, error) {\n   535→\toriginalJSON, err := obj.MarshalJSON()\n   536→\tif err != nil {\n   537→\t\treturn nil, err\n   538→\t}\n   539→\tvar patchedJSON []byte\n   540→\tif versionedObject == nil {\n   541→\t\tpatchedJSON, err = jsonpatch.MergePatch(originalJSON, 547→\t}\n   548→\n   549→\tpatchedObj := &unstructured.Unstructured{}\n   550→\t_, _, err = unstructured.UnstructuredJSONScheme.Decode(patchedJSON, nil, patchedObj)\n   551→\tif err != nil {\n been synced by another Application. If the resource is found in another Application it returns\n   559→// true along with a human readable message of which specific resource has this condition.\n   560→func hasSharedResourceCondition(app *v1alpha1.Application) (bool, string) {\n   561→\tfor _, condition := range app.Status.Conditions {\n   562→\t\tif condition.Type == v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionSharedResourceWarning {\n   563→\t\t\treturn true, condition.Message\n   564→\t\t}\n   565→\t}\n   566→\treturn false, \"\"\n   567→}\n   568→\n   569→// delayBetweenSyncWaves is a gitops-engine SyncWaveHook which introduces an artificial delay\n   570→// between each sync wave. We introduce an artificial delay in order give other controllers a\n   571→// _chance_ to react to the spec change that we just applied. This is important because without\n   572→// this, Argo CD will likely assess resource health too quickly (against the stale object), causing\n   573→// hooks to fire prematurely. See: https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/4669.\n   574→// Note, this is not foolproof, since a proper fix would require the CRD record\n   575→// status.observedGeneration coupled with a health.lua that verifies\n   576→// status.observedGeneration == metadata.generation\n   577→func delayBetweenSyncWaves(phase common.SyncPhase, wave int, finalWave bool) error {\n   578→\tif !finalWave {\n   579→\t\tdelaySec := 2\n   580→\t\tif delaySecStr := os.Getenv(EnvVarSyncWaveDelay); delaySecStr != \"\" {\n   581→\t\t\tif val, err := strconv.Atoi(delaySecStr); err == nil {\n   582→\t\t\t\tdelaySec = val\n   583→\t\t\t}\n   584→\t\t}\n   585→\t\tduration := time.Duration(delaySec) * time.Second\n   586→\t\ttime.Sleep(duration)\n   587→\t}\n   588→\treturn nil\n   589→}\n   590→\n   591→func syncWindowPreventsSync(app *v1alpha1.Application, proj *v1alpha1.AppProject) (bool, error) {\n   592→\twindow := proj.Spec.SyncWindows.Matches(app)\n   593→\tisManual := false\n   594→\tif app.Status.OperationState != nil {\n   595→\t\tisManual = !app.Status.OperationState.Operation.InitiatedBy.Automated\n   596→\t}\n   597→\tcanSync, err := window.CanSync(isManual)\n   598→\tif err != nil {\n   599→\t\t// prevents sync because sync window has an error\n   600→\t\treturn true, err\n   601→\t}\n   602→\treturn !canSync, nil\n   603→}\n   604→\n   605→// deriveServiceAccountToImpersonate determines the service account to be used for impersonation for the sync operation.\n   606→// The returned service account will be fully qualified including namespace and the service account name in the format system:serviceaccount:<namespace>:<service_account>\n   607→func deriveServiceAccountToImpersonate(project *v1alpha1.AppProject, application *v1alpha1.Application) (string, error) {\n   608→\t// spec.Destination.Namespace is optional. If not specified, use the Application's\n   609→\t// namespace\n   610→\tserviceAccountNamespace := application.Spec.Destination.Namespace\n   611→\tif serviceAccountNamespace == \"\" {\n   612→\t\tserviceAccountNamespace = application.Namespace\n   613→\t}\n   614→\t// Loop through the destinationServiceAccounts and see if there is any destination that is a candidate.\n   615→\t// if so, return the service account specified for that destination.\n   616→\tfor _, item := range project.Spec.DestinationServiceAccounts {\n   617→\t\tdstServerMatched, err := 620→\t\t}\n   621→\t\tdstNamespaceMatched, err := glob.MatchWithError(item.Namespace, application.Spec.Destination.Namespace)\n   622→\t\tif err != nil {\n   623→\t\t\treturn \"\", \" \") == \"\" || strings.ContainsAny(item.DefaultServiceAccount, serviceAccountDisallowedCharSet) {\n   627→\t\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"default service account contains invalid chars '%s'\", item.DefaultServiceAccount)\n   628→\t\t\t} else if strings.Contains(item.DefaultServiceAccount, \":\") {\n   629→\t\t\t\t// service account is specified along with its namespace.\n   630→\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"system:serviceaccount:%s\", item.DefaultServiceAccount), nil\n   631→\t\t\t} else {\n   632→\t\t\t\t// service account needs to be prefixed with a namespace\n   633→\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"system:serviceaccount:%s:%s\", serviceAccountNamespace, item.DefaultServiceAccount), nil\n   634→\t\t\t}\n   635→\t\t}\n   636→\t}\n   637→\t// if there is no match found in the AppProject.Spec.DestinationServiceAccounts, use the default service account of the destination namespace.\n   638→\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"no matching service account found for destination server %s and namespace %s\", application.Spec.Destination.Server, serviceAccountNamespace)\n   639→}\n   640→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive analysis report.\n\n## Argo CD Sync and Status Tracking: Comprehensive Analysis\n\nBased on my investigation of the Argo CD codebase, here's a detailed explanation of how Argo CD applies changes to the cluster and tracks sync status:\n\n---\n\n### 1. SYNC PHASES (PreSync, Sync, PostSync) AND WAVES ORCHESTRATION\n\n#### Phase Definitions\nArgo CD uses three distinct sync phases (from gitops-engine `synccommon.SyncPhase`):\n\n- **PreSync**: Executes before main resource application. Used for setup tasks like database migrations or data preparation.\n- **Sync**: The main phase where resources are applied to the cluster.\n- **PostSync**: Executes after main resources are synced. Used for post-deployment validation or cleanup.\n\n**File Reference**: `/workspace/test/e2e/hook_test.go` demonstrates these phases with resource hooks.\n\n#### Wave Orchestration\nWaves are executed sequentially within each phase, creating a dependency chain:\n\n1. **Wave Extraction**: Resources are ordered by examining the `argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave` annotation\n   - **File**: `/workspace/controller/sort_delete.go` contains `syncWaveSorter` that reads `syncwaves.Wave(obj)`\n   - Default wave is 0 if annotation is absent\n\n2. **Wave Delay Mechanism**:\n   ```go\n   // From /workspace/controller/sync.go:576-589\n   func delayBetweenSyncWaves(phase common.SyncPhase, wave int, finalWave bool) error {\n       if !finalWave {\n           delaySec := 2  // Default 2 second delay\n           if delaySecStr := os.Getenv(EnvVarSyncWaveDelay); delaySecStr != \"\" {\n               // Configurable via ARGOCD_SYNC_WAVE_DELAY\n           }\n           time.Sleep(duration)\n       }\n   }\n   ```\n   \n   This delay allows other controllers to react to spec changes before Argo CD assesses resource health (see issue #4669).\n\n3. **Wave Sorting in Deletion**: Resources are pruned in **reverse order**\n   ```go\n   // From /workspace/controller/sort_delete.go:29\n   sort.Sort(sort.Reverse(syncWaveSorter(objs)))\n   ```\n   This ensures dependencies are deleted in correct order (e.g., Pod before RoleBinding before Role).\n\n---\n\n### 2. CLIENT-SIDE VS SERVER-SIDE APPLY\n\n#### Server-Side Apply (SSA) Configuration\n\n**Enablement**:\n```go\n// From /workspace/controller/sync.go:381\nsync.WithServerSideApply(syncOp.SyncOptions.HasOption(common.SyncOptionServerSideApply))\n```\n\n**Key Differences**:\n\n| Aspect | Client-Side Apply | Server-Side Apply |\n|--------|-------------------|-------------------|\n| **Implementation** | kubectl patch/update locally | kubectl apply --server-side |\n| **Conflict Resolution** | Last-write-wins | Field ownership tracking |\n| **Marker** | `kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration` annotation | Field-level metadata |\n| **Structured Merge** | Uses JSON patch | Uses structured merge patches |\n| **Namespace Creation** | Adds tracking annotation | No tracking annotation (SSA enforced) |\n\n**Related Code**:\n- `/workspace/controller/state.go:754-756`: Enables structured merge diff when SSA is enabled\n- `/workspace/controller/sync_namespace.go:48-55`: SSA annotation enforcement for managed namespaces\n- `/workspace/test/e2e/app_sync_options_test.go:23-61`: Test demonstrates SSA with namespace creation\n\n**SSA Manager**: Uses `cdcommon.ArgoCDSSAManager` as the field manager identifier\n\n---\n\n### 3. RESOURCE ORDERING LOGIC\n\n#### Ordering Determination\n\n**Source of Order**:\n1. **Sync Wave Annotation**: Primary ordering mechanism\n   ```go\n   // From /workspace/controller/state.go:799\n   resState.SyncWave = int64(syncwaves.Wave(targetObj))\n   ```\n\n2. **Resource Filtering**: Applied during comparison phase\n   ```go\n   // From /workspace/controller/state.go:695\n   reconciliation := sync.Reconcile(targetObjs, liveObjByKey, \n                                    app.Spec.Destination.Namespace, infoProvider)\n   ```\n\n#### Ordering Pipeline\n\n1. **Target Objects Compilation** (`CompareAppState`):\n   - Get manifests from repo server\n   - Deduplicate resources\n   - Filter excluded resources\n   - Augment with managed namespace metadata\n\n2. **Live State Matching** (`sync.Reconcile`):\n   - Maps target objects to live cluster objects\n   - Creates index of resources by (Group, Kind, Namespace, Name)\n\n3. **Comparison and Wave Assignment** (`CompareAppState`):\n   - Iterates through target objects in order\n   - Assigns SyncWave from annotation (default 0)\n   - Marks sync status (Synced/OutOfSync)\n\n4. **Sync Execution** (`sync.NewSyncContext`):\n   - gitops-engine processes `ReconciliationResult` which contains ordered Target and Live arrays\n   - Resources applied according to wave ordering\n\n---\n\n### 4. SYNC STATUS TRACKING AND PROPAGATION\n\n#### Status Tracking Pipeline\n\n**Phase 1: Comparison (CompareAppState)**\n\n```go\n// From /workspace/controller/state.go:920-951\ncompRes := comparisonResult{\n    syncStatus:           &syncStatus,      // Overall sync state\n    healthStatus:         healthStatus,     // Resource health\n    resources:            resourceSummaries, // Per-resource status\n    managedResources:     managedResources,\n    reconciliationResult: reconciliation,   // Target/Live resources\n    diffConfig:           diffConfig,\n    diffResultList:       diffResults,\n}\n```\n\n**Per-Resource Status Assignment** (`/workspace/controller/state.go:771-840`):\n```go\nfor i, targetObj := range reconciliation.Target {\n    liveObj := reconciliation.Live[i]\n    \n    // Build ResourceStatus\n    resState := v1alpha1.ResourceStatus{\n        Namespace:       obj.GetNamespace(),\n        Name:            obj.GetName(),\n        Kind:            gvk.Kind,\n        SyncWave:        int64(syncwaves.Wave(targetObj)),\n        Status:          v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeOutOfSync, // or Synced\n        RequiresPruning: targetObj == nil && liveObj != nil,\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Phase 2: Sync Execution (SyncAppState)**\n\n```go\n// From /workspace/controller/sync.go:390-416\nsyncCtx, cleanup, err := sync.NewSyncContext(\n    compareResult.syncStatus.Revision,\n    reconciliationResult,     // Target and Live resources\n    restConfig,\n    rawConfig,\n    m.kubectl,\n    app.Spec.Destination.Namespace,\n    openAPISchema,\n    opts...,  // Includes sync wave hook, SSA flag, etc.\n)\n\nif state.Phase == common.OperationTerminating {\n    syncCtx.Terminate()\n} else {\n    syncCtx.Sync()  // Execute sync via gitops-engine\n}\n\n// Extract results\nstate.Phase, state.Message, resState = syncCtx.GetState()\n```\n\n**Resource Result Extraction**:\n```go\n// From /workspace/controller/sync.go:415-453\nvar resState []common.ResourceSyncResult\nstate.Phase, state.Message, resState = syncCtx.GetState()\n\nfor _, res := range resState {\n    state.SyncResult.Resources = append(state.SyncResult.Resources, res.SyncPhase,\n            Status:    res.Status,\n            Message:   res.Message,\n        })\n}\n```\n\n#### Status Persistence\n\n**Step 1: setOperationState() - Operation State Update**\n\n```go\n// From /workspace/controller/appcontroller.go:1501-1575\nfunc (ctrl *ApplicationController) setOperationState(app *appv1.Application, \n                                                    state *appv1.OperationState) {\n    if state.Phase.Completed() {\n        now := metav1.Now()\n        state.FinishedAt = &now\n    }\n    \n    patch := map[string]interface{}{\n        \"status\": map[string]interface{}{\n            \"operationState\": state,\n        },\n    }\n    \n    if state.Phase.Completed() {\n        patch[\"operation\"] = nil  // Clear operation to indicate completion\n    }\n    \n    // Patch application with merge strategy\n    ctrl.PatchAppWithWriteBack(context.Background(), app.Name, app.Namespace, \n                               types.MergePatchType, patchJSON, ...)\n}\n```\n\n**State Structure**:\n```go\n// From /workspace/pkg/apis/application/v1alpha1/types.go:1369-1384\ntype OperationState struct {\n    Operation Operation              `json:\"operation\"`\n    Phase synccommon.OperationPhase  `json:\"phase\"`\n    Message string                   `json:\"message,omitempty\"`\n    SyncResult *SyncOperationResult  `json:\"syncResult,omitempty\"`\n    StartedAt metav1.Time            `json:\"startedAt\"`\n    FinishedAt *metav1.Time          `json:\"finishedAt,omitempty\"`\n    RetryCount int64                 `json:\"retryCount,omitempty\"`\n}\n```\n\n**Step 2: Operation Queuing and Processing**\n\n```go\n// From /workspace/controller/appcontroller.go:1365-1440\nfunc (ctrl *ApplicationController) processRequestedAppOperation(app *appv1.Application) {\n    state := &appv1.OperationState{\n        Phase: synccommon.OperationRunning,\n        Operation: *app.Operation,\n        StartedAt: metav1.Now(),\n    }\n    ctrl.setOperationState(app, state)\n    \n    // Execute sync\n    ctrl.appStateManager.SyncAppState(app, state)\n    \n    // Update final state\n    ctrl.setOperationState(app, state)\n}\n```\n\n**Step 3: Revision History Persistence**\n\n```go\n// From /workspace/controller/sync.go:457-463\nif !syncOp.DryRun && len(syncOp.Resources) == 0 &&                       compareResult.syncStatus.ComparedTo.Sources,\n                                    isMultiSourceRevision, state.StartedAt, \n                                    state.Operation.InitiatedBy)\n}\n```\n\n#### Status Propagation Back to Application\n\nThe complete flow from operation to application status:\n\n```\nApplication.Operation (user request)\n    ↓\nprocessRequestedAppOperation()\n    ├→ Create OperationState with Phase=Running\n    ├→ setOperationState() [patches app.status.operationState]\n    ├→ SyncAppState()\n    │   ├→ CompareAppState() [builds comparison with per-resource sync status]\n    │   ├→ sync.NewSyncContext() [initializes gitops-engine context]\n    │   ├→ syncCtx.Sync() [executes actual apply]\n    │   └→ Extract ResourceSyncResults\n    ├→ Map results to ResourceResult array\n    ├→ Update state.SyncResult.Resources\n    └→ setOperationState() [final patch with complete results]\n        └→ Application.Status.OperationState contains:\n            - Phase (Succeeded/Failed/Running)\n            - SyncResult with per-resource results\n            - FinishedAt timestamp\n            - Revision/Revisions synced to\n```\n\n#### Key Status Fields in ResourceResult\n\n```go\n// From /workspace/pkg/apis/application/v1alpha1/types.go:1600-1622\ntype ResourceResult struct {\n    Group string                      // API group\n    Kind string                       // Resource kind\n    Namespace string                  // Namespace\n    Name string                       // Resource name\n    Status synccommon.ResultCode      // Synced/OutOfSync/Unknown\n    Message string                    // Status message\n    HookType synccommon.HookType      // PreSync/Sync/PostSync/SyncFail/PostDelete\n    HookPhase synccommon.OperationPhase // Succeeded/Failed for hooks\n    SyncPhase synccommon.SyncPhase    // PreSync/Sync/PostSync phase executed\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. SYNC OPTIONS CONFIGURATION\n\n**Available Sync Options** (passed during operation):\n- `ServerSideApply=true`: Use server-side apply\n- `CreateNamespace=true`: Auto-create namespace\n- `PruneLast`: Delete resources after creating new ones\n- `Replace`: Replace resources instead of merge\n- `ApplyOutOfSyncOnly=true`: Only apply modified resources\n- `DisableValidation`: Skip manifest validation\n- `FailOnSharedResource=true`: Fail if shared resources detected\n- `DeleteRequireConfirm`: Require confirmation for deletion\n- `RespectIgnoreDifferences=true`: Apply ignore differences to sync\n\n---\n\n### 6. Update**\n\n```go\n// From /workspace/controller/appcontroller.go:1501-1575\nfunc (ctrl *ApplicationController) setOperationState(app *appv1.Application, \n                                                    state *appv1.OperationState) {\n    if state.Phase.Completed() {\n        now := metav1.Now()\n        state.FinishedAt = &now\n    }\n    \n    patch := map[string]interface{}{\n        \"status\": map[string]interface{}{\n            \"operationState\": state,\n        },\n    }\n    \n    if state.Phase.Completed() {\n        patch[\"operation\"] = nil  // Clear operation to indicate completion\n    }\n    \n    // Patch application with merge strategy\n    ctrl.PatchAppWithWriteBack(context.Background(), app.Name, app.Namespace, \n                               types.MergePatchType, patchJSON, ...)\n}\n```\n\n**State Structure**:\n```go\n// From /workspace/pkg/apis/application/v1alpha1/types.go:1369-1384\ntype OperationState struct {\n    Operation Operation metav1.Now(),\n    }\n    ctrl.setOperationState(app, state)\n    \n    // Execute sync\n    ctrl.appStateManager.SyncAppState(app, state)\n    \n    // Update final state\n    ctrl.setOperationState(app, state)\n}\n```\n\n**Step 3: Revision History Persistence**\n\n```go\n// From /workspace/controller/sync.go:457-463\nif !syncOp.DryRun && len(syncOp.Resources) == 0 &&                       compareResult.syncStatus.ComparedTo.Sources,\n                                    isMultiSourceRevision, state.StartedAt, \n                                    state.Operation.InitiatedBy)\n}\n```\n\n#### Status Propagation Back to Application\n\nThe complete flow from operation to application status:\n\n```\nApplication.Operation (user request)\n    ↓\nprocessRequestedAppOperation()\n    ├→ Create OperationState with Phase=Running\n    ├→ setOperationState() [patches app.status.operationState]\n    ├→ SyncAppState()\n    │   ├→ CompareAppState() name\n    Status synccommon.ResultCode      // Synced/OutOfSync/Unknown\n    Message string                    // Status message\n    HookType synccommon.HookType      // executed\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. SYNC OPTIONS CONFIGURATION\n\n**Available Sync Options** (passed during operation):\n- `ServerSideApply=true`: Use server-side apply\n- `CreateNamespace=true`: Auto-create namespace\n- `PruneLast`: Delete resources after creating new ones\n- `Replace`: Replace resources instead of merge\n- `ApplyOutOfSyncOnly=true`: Only apply modified resources\n- `DisableValidation`: Skip manifest validation\n- `FailOnSharedResource=true`: Fail if shared resources detected\n- `DeleteRequireConfirm`: Require confirmation for deletion\n- `RespectIgnoreDifferences=true`: Apply ignore differences to sync\n\n---\n\n### 6. ok`\n   - Supports Normal or Hard refresh types\n   - File: `/workspace/controller/appcontroller.go:1895`\n\n5. **Live State Drift Detection (Related Resource Changes)**\n   - LiveStateCache detects changes to managed resources\n   - Calls `handleObjectUpdated()` with appropriate refresh level\n   - File: `/workspace/controller/appcontroller.go:419-479`\n\n### Controller-to-RepoServer Communication\n\n**Primary Method**: `GetRepoObjs()` → `repoClient.GenerateManifest()`\n\n**Request Flow**:\n1. File: `/workspace/controller/state.go:269`\n\n**Detection Method**: `needRefreshAppStatus(app, statusRefreshTimeout, statusHardRefreshTimeout) (bool, RefreshType, CompareWith)`\n- Checks timeout
